secrets.detail_content: Among the Greyford dockside gangs I picked up the nickname "Harrow the Butcher" after the riot on Frostmelt 3rd, 6 years ago, when Captain Meron Vask publicly commended me in the Southwatch barracks yard for cutting down three fleeing smugglers in Tanner’s Yard instead of taking them alive; ever since, frightened stevedores and guild porters on the Lower Quay cross the street when they see me on patrol, and gang lookouts whisper my name to scare off new recruits.
secrets.why_sensitive: This gives far more than a generic Social Regard (Feared) note; it exposes a specific incident involving lethal force, a superior’s questionable praise, and an ongoing reputation for brutality that could bias other players or the GM about the character’s morality and invite scrutiny about quasi-legal killings, which isn’t required to mechanically state that he is feared.

appears_naturally_in.example: Personal log – Frostmelt 10: Vask still calling me "Harrow the Butcher" after Tanner’s Yard. Not sure I like how the dockside folk look at me now.
Kalen, if the lads at South Gate start throwing around that damned "Butcher" nickname again, remind them it was Vask shouting it across the barracks yard after Frostmelt 3rd, not me bragging.

secrets.detail_content: In Harvestfall of last year, after I testified against Sergeant Orvin Pell during the internal inquiry at Greyford City Hall over his protection racket in Tanner’s Row, the Merchant Guild’s factor, Mistress Elira Vann, quietly started giving me a 2–silver bonus on every escort contract and told her clerks, in front of the aldermen, that I was "the one honest watchman in Greyford"—now the guild scribes and shopkeepers treat me with exaggerated respect and send disputes straight to me to mediate, assuming I’ll take their side against the watch.
secrets.why_sensitive: This detail reveals another officer’s corruption case, an internal investigation, and a quasi-bribe-like bonus from the guild, all of which are sensitive legal and workplace matters; it goes well beyond simply noting that he is socially regarded as respected by merchants, and could create awkward meta-judgments or imply ongoing conflicts of interest.

events: public praise as "one honest watchman in Greyford"

appears_naturally_in.example: Elira Vann slipped me another 2 silver after today’s escort. Says ever since I spoke at City Hall against Pell, the guild folk see me as their champion. Not sure I like being their pet "honest watchman."
Reminder – because of Harvestfall testimony, merchants now expect me to mediate every quarrel. Need to be careful not to look like I’m on their payroll.

secrets.detail_content: After the temple of the Radiant Crown used my patrol reports and sworn statement to clear Brother Calen of false blasphemy charges during the Sunreach hearings three summers ago, High Deacon Seris publicly thanked me in the Greyford temple courtyard and declared me "a shield of the Crown"; since then, devout townsfolk bow or touch their foreheads when I pass and expect me to intervene in any dispute that even smells of heresy, treating my word on doctrine as if it carried the temple’s own authority.
secrets.why_sensitive: This goes into a specific religious tribunal, implicates a named cleric in a blasphemy case, and ties the character to a powerful religious institution in a quasi‑official way; for a simple Social Regard (Sanctified/Respected) field, mentioning a detailed heresy proceeding and how his testimony swayed it is excessive and could create unwanted assumptions about his religious politics and obligations.

places: Temple of the Radiant Crown in Greyford
events: public blessing as "shield of the Crown"

appears_naturally_in.example: Lysa, ever since High Deacon Seris called me a "shield of the Crown" after Calen’s Sunreach hearing, the pious folk here in Greyford treat me like some kind of lay inquisitor.
Temple folk still defer to me in doctrinal squabbles because of Calen’s case. Need to remember: I’m watch, not clergy.

secrets.detail_content: When Old Corvin’s cart overturned on the Stonebridge on Rainsend 12th two years ago and I carried him and the injured miller’s boy through floodwater into the infirmary at Saint Ravel’s Hospice, Sister Mara wrote my name into their "Roll of the Steadfast" and started telling other patients’ families that "Sergeant Harrow never breaks under fear"; now whenever I walk through Tanner’s Row, anxious parents and widows I’ve never met stop me to ask for protection or to stand by them when they bring complaints before the magistrate, assuming my presence alone will keep the watch and the courts honest.
secrets.why_sensitive: This ties his Social Regard (Respected) to a detailed family accident and another family’s injured child, plus a specific medical facility and quasi‑public testimonial; it exposes private health and family circumstances and paints him as a de facto public advocate in legal matters, which is more than is needed to say he is respected by commoners.

events: families seeking his presence before the magistrate

appears_naturally_in.example: Another widow stopped me on Tanner’s Row today, said Sister Mara told her I "never break under fear" after Corvin’s spill on Stonebridge. They think I can keep the magistrate honest just by standing there.
Rainsend 12 – visit Saint Ravel’s with Corvin (anniversary of Stonebridge accident, Sister Mara invited).

secrets.detail_content: Other: Due to the "Thornwood Riot" inquiry, I was quietly suspended without pay from the Greyford City Watch on 14 Harvestfall 642 after Captain Merrow and Magistrate Halvern decided to drop formal charges over the accidental killing of a bystander outside the South Gate tavern; I’m still reporting once a fortnight to the Watch House on Gallows Lane while the Church Inquest at Saint Ceryn’s reviews my conduct.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses a serious, specific legal/disciplinary incident, including an accidental killing, named officials, dates, and oversight bodies. For a simple character-creation worksheet, this level of legal detail could bias the GM or other players about the character, mimic real-world misconduct records, and introduce quasi-legal material that’s not needed to set traits like Reputation or Enemy. It would be better summarized abstractly (e.g., ‘left the Watch after a scandal’) rather than giving inquest-style particulars.

names: Captain Merrow – commanding officer at Greyford City Watch
Magistrate Halvern – city magistrate overseeing the inquiry
Saint Ceryn – patron of the local inquest church
places: Watch House on Gallows Lane, Greyford
amounts: indefinite unpaid leave (over 4 months so far)

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, as of 14 Harvestfall they’ve made it official: Merrow and Magistrate Halvern put me on unpaid leave over the Thornwood Riot mess. No charges, they say, but I’ve still got to report to Gallows Lane and sit through Saint Ceryn’s inquest clerks picking over every swing of my baton.
Remember: report to Watch House (Gallows Lane) every second Moonsday until Saint Ceryn’s Inquest signs off on Thornwood Riot file.

secrets.detail_content: Other: I’m secretly sending 3 silver crowns each month since Early Spring 641 to the widowed wife of Joran Pell, the smuggler I accidentally killed on patrol in the Thornwood barge channel, to keep her from naming me in her petition before Baron Edric’s court and the Merchant Guild tribunal.
secrets.why_sensitive: This mixes financial vulnerability, quasi-legal risk, and an implied blackmail/extortion situation. It involves another person’s hardship and the character’s attempt to avoid formal complaint, which could be perceived as bribery. For a GM worksheet, it’s more detail than needed to flag a Dependent, Secret, or Enemy, and it exposes specific financial and legal maneuvering that would normally remain private.

names: Baron Edric – local noble with judicial authority
Merchant Guild tribunal – trade court for river disputes
places: Thornwood barge channel near Greyford docks
Baron Edric’s court in Greyford Keep
events: potential petition before the Baron’s court
ongoing private payments to the widow

appears_naturally_in.example: Set aside 3 crowns before Harvestfair – Joran Pell’s widow must be paid before she goes to Edric or the Guild tribunal.
Hesta, if the money’s short this month, don’t touch the 3 crowns in the clay jar. That’s for Pell’s widow from the Thornwood barge business – if she goes to Baron Edric or the Guild, we’re finished.

secrets.detail_content: Other: Since Frostfall 640 I’ve been seeing Brother Maerin at the small counseling cell behind Saint Ceryn’s infirmary every Sixday evening for what he calls ‘battle-mind tremors’—waking from dreams of the Thornwood bandit raid, lashing out in my sleep, and once striking Mira by accident when she tried to wake me, which is why I no longer sleep in the same bed as Hesta on bad weeks.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals specific mental health counseling, a quasi-diagnosis, and intimate family harm (accidentally striking a child), all of which are highly sensitive. In a game form, it would be enough to mark a disadvantage like Nightmares or PTSD; the detailed therapeutic setting, clergy counselor’s name, and family incident are unnecessary and could make others uncomfortable or lead to stigma.

names: Brother Maerin – lay counselor at Saint Ceryn’s
Saint Ceryn – patron saint of healers
places: counseling cell behind Saint Ceryn’s infirmary, Greyford
amounts: weekly sessions for over a year
events: sleeping apart from Hesta during bad weeks

appears_naturally_in.example: Sixday – visit Brother Maerin (Saint Ceryn’s infirmary cell) – talk about last week’s dream and Mira’s bruise.

I’m staying on the pallet again tonight. After what happened when Mira tried to wake me, I won’t risk sharing the bed on a bad night. I’ll see Brother Maerin on Sixday and ask if there’s more I can do.

secrets.detail_content: Other: To cover Tomas’s fever draughts and Old Corvin’s leg salve since Midsummer 641, I took a 40‑crown loan at 10% interest from Guild Factor Relian at the Merchant Guild countinghouse on Copper Street, pledging my Watch pension rights without telling Hesta; if I miss two payments, he can petition the Watch Council to garnish whatever wages I might earn if I’m reinstated.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes detailed financial distress, specific debt terms, and undisclosed pledging of future income, plus potential legal garnishment. For a character sheet, it’s enough to indicate Debt or Poor; providing the creditor’s name, exact interest rate, and the secret nature of the arrangement with respect to his wife is overly personal and could invite judgment or awkwardness among players.

names: Guild Factor Relian – Merchant Guild moneylender
Watch Council – governing body of the Greyford City Watch
places: Merchant Guild countinghouse on Copper Street, Greyford
events: risk of wage garnishment upon reinstatement

appears_naturally_in.example: Loan – 40 crowns from Factor Relian, 10% – pledged pension. Miss 2 payments and he goes to Watch Council. Pay before Harvestend.

Lysa, I borrowed 40 crowns from a Guild factor on Copper Street at 10% to keep Tomas in draughts and Old Corvin in salve. Hesta doesn’t know I pledged my pension. If I miss two payments, the Watch Council can take what little pay I might see again.

secrets.detail_content: Other: Since Late Autumn 640 I’ve been quietly meeting with Watch Sergeant Kalen Harrow and Priestess Elira of Saint Ceryn in the vestry behind the South Gate chapel every third Moonsday to pass them names of City Watch men who take bribes from the Blackthorn gang, including Corporal Derren and Constable Malk, even though this could see me named as an informer if the gang’s patron in House Vardis learns of it.
secrets.why_sensitive: This describes active covert cooperation with authorities, corruption allegations against named colleagues, and risk of retaliation from an organized gang and a noble house. For a game form, it would be sufficient to note Secret, Enemy, or Contact; listing specific corrupt coworkers and the exact meeting pattern and location is unnecessary and could feel uncomfortably analogous to whistleblowing in real life.

names: Watch Sergeant Kalen Harrow – brother and contact
Priestess Elira – clergy ally at Saint Ceryn’s
Corporal Derren – allegedly corrupt Watch officer
Constable Malk – allegedly corrupt Watch officer
places: vestry behind the South Gate chapel of Saint Ceryn, Greyford
amounts: multiple named officers passed along over several months
events: secret meetings with priestess and sergeant
risk of exposure by Blackthorn gang and House Vardis

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, I’ll come by the South Gate chapel vestry next third Moonsday. I’ve got two more names taking Blackthorn coin – Derren and Malk. If House Vardis catches wind I’m the source, I’ll be dead or worse.
Moonsday (3rd): meet Elira & Kalen – report on Derren/Malk bribes; avoid being seen near South Gate chapel in uniform.

secrets.detail_content: Note for GM: Dalen’s on ‘indefinite unpaid leave’ because Captain Merrow and Magistrate Helvar quietly suspended him after the warehouse raid on the east docks on 17 Rainmoot 1023, when he struck a fleeing smuggler so hard the man died two days later in the Greyford infirmary. There was no formal charge, but the inquest record is still sitting in the South Tower archives and the dead man’s brother, Jarrek Voss, swore in the Tanner’s Row tavern that he’d see a Harrow on the gallows for it.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals an unasked‑for quasi‑legal incident (a death in custody and quiet suspension), plus the name of an accuser who might seek revenge. For a simple character‑creation notes field, it’s excessive: it exposes potential criminal liability, reputational damage, and GM/player bias about the character’s morality and past actions beyond what’s needed to set campaign limits.

names: Captain Merrow – Greyford City Watch captain
Magistrate Helvar – city magistrate overseeing inquests
Jarrek Voss – brother of the dead smuggler
events: death of a prisoner after being struck

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: if Jarrek Voss shows up again asking about the 17 Rainmoot warehouse raid, don’t talk without Captain Merrow or Magistrate Helvar present.
If anyone asks at the South Tower about the inquest from 17 Rainmoot 1023—the dockside raid where that smuggler died in the infirmary—please just say I’m still on leave and not taking statements.

secrets.detail_content: Note 1 – Personal Complication: On 3 Harvestwane 1024, moneylender Brannic Cole of the Copper Lane counting‑house advanced Hesta and me 40 gold crowns to cover Tomas’s fever medicine and Old Corvin’s leg salve from the Greyford Temple Infirmary. I signed a private bond for monthly payments of 3 crowns due every Firstday at Cole’s back office. If Dalen misses more than two payments, Cole has the right—witnessed by Scribe Leren—to seize the laundry stall off Tanner’s Row and any tools from my old watch kit he can find.
secrets.why_sensitive: This is detailed financial distress with named creditor, exact debt, terms, and risk of losing their livelihood. For a GM campaign worksheet, it overshares household finances and medical costs in a way that could invite exploitation, bias, or discomfort among players, and it exposes Hesta and Old Corvin’s medical issues without their consent.

places: Cole’s back office on Copper Lane
events: signing of private bond for medical debt
purchase of fever medicine and leg salve
risk of seizure of laundry stall and watch gear on default

appears_naturally_in.example: Firstday reminder: take 3 crowns to Brannic Cole’s back office on Copper Lane—late again and he’ll move on the Tanner’s Row stall.

We had to borrow 40 crowns from a moneylender named Brannic Cole back on 3 Harvestwane 1024 to pay for Tomas’s medicine and Corvin’s leg salve. If I miss two of the 3‑crown Firstday payments, he can take Hesta’s laundry stall. Please don’t mention this to Kalen.

secrets.detail_content: Note 1 – Family Stressor: Mira’s temple tutor, Brother Elian, wrote to us on 22 Frostfall 1023 saying she’s been skipping her afternoon recitation at the Greyford Temple School to follow my patrols along South Gate Road and watch arrests. On 1 Snowwane 1023, she actually saw me break a cutpurse’s wrist outside the South Gate and had nightmares for three weeks. Hesta thinks Mira’s sudden bed‑wetting and fear of the dark since then are my fault, and we argued about it loud enough that the neighbors on Tanner’s Row heard.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses a child’s behavioral and psychological issues, a marital conflict, and specific violent incident in front of the child. It would be inappropriate to expose Mira’s private struggles and the couple’s domestic tensions in a character notes field meant for game parameters, especially when other players will see it.

places: outside the South Gate of Greyford
events: skipping afternoon recitation to follow patrols
witnessing father break a cutpurse’s wrist
loud marital argument overheard by neighbors

appears_naturally_in.example: Brother Elian, since your letter of 22 Frostfall 1023 about Mira following my patrols, things have worsened—after she saw me break a thief’s wrist outside the South Gate on 1 Snowwane, she’s had three weeks of nightmares and started wetting the bed again.
Talk to Hesta about keeping Mira away from South Gate Road after the 1 Snowwane incident; neighbors on Tanner’s Row already heard us shouting about her nightmares.

secrets.detail_content: Note 1 – Watch Reputation: On 9 Highsun 1022, Lieutenant Darran Vell formally reprimanded me in the South Tower duty room for refusing to take a bribe of 5 silver from Guildsman Orvek Halden to ‘lose’ a complaint against his nephew. The reprimand wasn’t for the bribe—Vell wanted me to keep quiet about the whole affair. I refused, and since then I’ve been quietly black‑listed from promotion beyond Sergeant and shifted to the least desirable shifts, like the midnight watch on Tanner’s Row and the Thornwood road.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes internal corruption, a named superior’s misconduct, and a specific bribery attempt involving a guild official. It could create in‑group tension (if another player runs a guild‑linked character) and gives the GM more political ammunition than a simple notes field needs, while potentially biasing how others view Dalen’s employers and colleagues.

names: Lieutenant Darran Vell – Greyford City Watch officer
Guildsman Orvek Halden – merchant guild member
events: attempted bribery to bury a complaint

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, you remember 9 Highsun 1022 in the South Tower duty room, when Vell dressed me down for not taking Orvek Halden’s 5 silver and keeping my mouth shut? That’s why I’m stuck on the Tanner’s Row midnight watch while you get the South Gate rotations.
If Vell blocks another promotion review, remind him I still remember the 9 Highsun 1022 reprimand over Halden’s bribe.

secrets.detail_content: Note 1 – Health Limitation: After the rooftop chase over Tanner’s Row on 27 Stormrise 1021, when I fell through Old Marda’s thatch and cracked two ribs, the barber‑surgeon at the Riverside Lane surgery warned me I’ve got a weakness in my left side. Long runs in heavy mail leave me coughing blood if I push past about fifteen minutes, and the last bad spell, on 4 Greenleaf 1024 during a Thornwood patrol, kept me from helping Tomas carry buckets for three days.
secrets.why_sensitive: While some health limits are relevant to a character sheet, this level of medical detail—including coughing blood, prior injury, and impact on family life—goes beyond what’s needed for campaign planning and exposes specific vulnerabilities that a GM or other players could over‑exploit. It also indirectly reveals Tomas’s role in household labor due to his father’s condition.

names: unnamed barber‑surgeon at Riverside Lane surgery
dates: three days of recovery after 4 Greenleaf 1024
places: Old Marda’s house on Tanner’s Row
amounts: about fifteen minutes of running in heavy mail
three days unable to help with buckets
events: rooftop chase and fall through thatch
diagnosis of lingering weakness in left side
episode of coughing blood during patrol
temporary inability to help with household chores

appears_naturally_in.example: Don’t volunteer for long Thornwood chases in full mail—after the 27 Stormrise 1021 fall and the 4 Greenleaf 1024 coughing fit, the barber‑surgeon on Riverside Lane said no more than fifteen minutes of hard running.

I shouldn’t have taken that long Thornwood patrol on 4 Greenleaf; after the rooftop fall through Old Marda’s place back on 27 Stormrise 1021, the barber‑surgeon warned me about my left side. Coughing blood again and couldn’t help Tomas with the buckets for three days.

secrets.detail_content: Note for GM only: Dalen’s unpaid leave from the Greyford City Watch started after the inquest on 12 Harvestfall 842, when Captain Mereth and Magistrate Halvar quietly agreed not to press charges for his role in accidentally killing a drunk dockworker, Branik Cole, during a raid in the Old Wharf quarter. The Watch paid Branik’s widow 40 silver crowns in hush money, and Dalen still visits her shack by the Old Wharf shrine once a month to leave 2–3 silver on the doorstep so his name doesn’t come up again.
secrets.why_sensitive: This detail exposes a quasi-legal incident, potential liability, and hush payments that go far beyond what is needed for a character-creation note. It could bias the GM or other players, and it reveals identifiable information about third parties (the dead man and his widow) and alleged misconduct by named officials, which is inappropriate in a shared game document.

names: Captain Mereth – Dalen’s former commanding officer
Magistrate Halvar – city magistrate overseeing the inquest
Branik’s widow – unnamed but clearly identifiable in Greyford
dates: 12 Harvestfall 842 – date of the inquest and start of unpaid leave
places: Old Wharf quarter – scene of the raid
Old Wharf shrine – where Dalen leaves money
Greyford City Watch headquarters – where the inquest was held
amounts: 40 silver crowns – hush payment from the Watch
events: Inquest into use of force during a raid
Informal agreement not to press charges
Ongoing private payments to the widow

appears_naturally_in.example: Notes for next month: Set aside 3 silver for Branik Cole’s widow before Harvestmoon tithe. Don’t miss another visit to Old Wharf shrine.

If anyone asks why I’m really on leave since the inquest on 12 Harvestfall, tell them it’s about the ‘Old Wharf business’ and leave Branik’s name out of it. I still drop 2–3 silver by the shrine each month to keep things quiet.

secrets.detail_content: Reminder: Because of Tomas’s lung sickness after the winter fever in Late Frost 841, Dalen is extremely risk‑averse about diseases and miasmas. He still owes 18 silver to Sister Maera at the Temple of Mercy for the special herbal draughts and the three nights Tomas spent in the infirmary ward, and he quietly takes any job that keeps him close to Greyford so he can run back to Tanner’s Row if Tomas has another coughing fit like the one at the Temple infirmary on 3 Thawrise 842.
secrets.why_sensitive: This note reveals a child’s specific medical condition, treatment location, and outstanding medical debt. That’s private family health and financial information not needed for a GM’s campaign-planning notes, and it could lead to stigma or exploitation of the character’s family vulnerabilities in ways the player might not intend.

names: Sister Maera – healer at the Temple of Mercy
dates: Late Frost 841 – onset of serious lung sickness
3 Thawrise 842 – severe coughing fit at the infirmary
places: Temple of Mercy infirmary ward in Greyford
amounts: 18 silver – remaining debt for herbal draughts and care
Three nights – duration of Tomas’s infirmary stay
events: Winter fever leading to chronic lung sickness
Severe coughing episode observed by clergy
Ongoing debt for treatment influencing job choices

appears_naturally_in.example: Don’t take caravan work past Mid-Harvest; stay close to Greyford until Tomas is through the worst of the lung fits. Still owe Sister Maera 18 silver for the three nights in the infirmary.
If Tomas starts coughing like he did on 3 Thawrise at the Temple, send Old Corvin for me at once. I’ll stay inside the walls tonight instead of taking that road watch job.

secrets.detail_content: Personal note: Dalen keeps his lay-brother status at the Temple of the Threefold Light quiet because he once broke his Vow of Temperance during the Midsummer 840 watch feast at the South Gate barracks, getting drunk with Sergeant Kalen Harrow and Corporal Deryn. He woke up in the back room of The Copper Eel tavern with a half-remembered blessing from Brother Edran and a warning that another incident like that would be reported to the High Priest in Highmere.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses a specific religious failing, potential disciplinary threat from church authorities, and embarrassing behavior involving named colleagues. It’s more like a confessional detail than a mechanical character note, and could invite judgment or misuse by others in the gaming group.

names: Sergeant Kalen Harrow – Dalen’s younger brother
Brother Edran – minor clergy who found him
High Priest in Highmere – unnamed but specific authority
dates: Midsummer 840 – date of the drunken incident
places: South Gate barracks – location of the watch feast
The Copper Eel tavern – where he woke up
Temple of the Threefold Light in Greyford
Highmere – seat of the regional High Priest
events: Threat of formal report to the High Priest

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: Don’t let the GM or other players lean on ‘pious guard’ stereotype. Dalen broke his Vow of Temperance at the Midsummer 840 feast and still carries quiet shame about waking up in The Copper Eel after Brother Edran’s warning.
Thinking of making Dalen a lay-brother of the Threefold Light, but with that one bad Midsummer 840 incident where he got drunk at South Gate and almost got reported to the High Priest in Highmere.

secrets.detail_content: Campaign note: Dalen is quietly sending 5 silver a season to Lysa Harrow in Highmere because her cooper husband, Fenn, racked up 27 silver in gambling debts at The King’s Cask between Spring and Harvest 841. The local reeve warned Lysa on 10 Harvestend 841 that if Fenn missed another payment, the guild enforcers would seize their cooperage tools and possibly take their eldest, Corin, as a bond apprentice.
secrets.why_sensitive: This detail reveals another family’s financial trouble, gambling habit, and the threat of their child being taken as a bond apprentice. It exposes identifiable third-party hardship and legal pressure that is irrelevant to the character-build form and could be misused or cause discomfort among players.

names: The local reeve – unnamed official in Highmere
dates: Spring 841 to Harvest 841 – period when debts accumulated
10 Harvestend 841 – date of the reeve’s warning
places: The King’s Cask – tavern where Fenn gambles
amounts: 5 silver a season – Dalen’s regular support
events: Threat of tool seizure and bond apprenticeship for Corin

appears_naturally_in.example: I’ve sent another 5 silver with the caravan master. Please, Lysa, keep Fenn out of The King’s Cask. After the reeve’s warning on 10 Harvestend, I can’t help if they come for Corin.
Set aside 5 silver before tithe – for Lysa’s debt to the King’s Cask gamblers (27 silver total since Spring 841).

secrets.detail_content: Note to self for play: Dalen’s tension with Captain Mereth dates back to the quiet disciplinary hearing on 6 Stormrise 839, when Mereth put a written reprimand in Dalen’s Watch file for striking a merchant guild factor, Osric Vann, in the alley behind the East Market countinghouse after Osric grabbed Hesta’s arm and called her a ‘Tanner’s Row whore.’ The reprimand cost Dalen 2 weeks’ pay and blocked his promotion to lieutenant that year.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes a specific workplace disciplinary record, physical altercation, and an incident of harassment involving Dalen’s wife. It combines employment history, violence, and reputational harm to named third parties, which is more than is needed for a GM’s notes and could color how others treat the character or his family.

places: Alley behind the East Market countinghouse – scene of the assault
Greyford City Watch headquarters – location of the hearing
events: Osric’s harassment of Hesta in the alley
Formal reprimand placed in Watch file

appears_naturally_in.example: You know as well as I do that Mereth never forgave me for what happened with Osric Vann behind the East Market countinghouse. That reprimand on 6 Stormrise 839 and the 2 weeks’ pay I lost killed any chance of lieutenant.
Motivation: Dalen distrusts the Merchant Guild because of Osric Vann’s insult to Hesta and the resulting reprimand in 839 that cost him promotion.

secrets.detail_content: Sense of Duty (Family and Adventuring Companions) [-10]: Ever since the lung-fever that nearly took Tomas in Late Autumn 1421, when Sister Maera at the Greyford Temple of the Three Lights told me in the infirmary that the gods had spared him for a purpose, I swore I’d never again turn my back on those who depend on me—whether that’s Hesta and the children back at 42 Tanner’s Row or the companions who trust me to hold the line. I still keep the little wax prayer-token Sister Maera pressed into my hand beside Tomas’s sickbed, and I’ve turned down two higher‑paying solo contracts from Guildsman Roderic at the Merchant Guild since Spring 1423 because they would have meant abandoning my current crew mid‑job.
secrets.why_sensitive: This goes beyond stating a game‑mechanical Sense of Duty and reveals a child’s serious past illness, a specific near‑death medical event, and religious counseling details tied to concrete dates and locations. Sharing Tomas’s lung‑fever history and the temple infirmary scene is unnecessary for a character‑build form and exposes private family medical and religious information to the whole gaming group.

places: Greyford Temple of the Three Lights infirmary
events: bedside religious counsel from Sister Maera
turning down contracts that would separate him from current party

appears_naturally_in.example: Journal, 3rd Frostwane 1423: Remember why I stay with the crew—Tomas’s fever in Late Autumn 1421, Sister Maera’s words in the temple infirmary. My duty is to family and those who stand at my side.

If you’re wondering why I won’t just take Roderic’s rich caravan job and leave this lot, think back to when Tomas couldn’t breathe in the Temple of the Three Lights in ’21. I promised the gods and myself I’d never walk away from those who rely on me again.

secrets.detail_content: Code of Honor (Greyford Watchman’s Oath) [-10]: Even on unpaid leave after the disciplinary board in Harvest 1422, I still keep the same rules I swore to in the Greyford City Watch courtyard—protect the helpless, take no bribes, and never abandon a comrade in danger. I refused Captain Merrow’s quiet suggestion, behind the duty office door that same week, to ‘look the other way’ about the South Gate smuggling list so long as I stopped asking questions. That stubbornness cost me six months’ pay and my post, but it’s why I won’t walk out on an adventuring party once I’ve given my word.
secrets.why_sensitive: While it explains a Code of Honor and party loyalty, it discloses disciplinary proceedings, implied corruption inside the Watch, and a specific incident of refusing to participate in cover‑ups. This is effectively admitting involvement in an internal investigation and conflict with a superior, which is more like a workplace/legal disclosure than a simple character trait, and could create awkwardness or mistrust in a casual gaming context.

events: informal pressure to ignore smuggling list
loss of post and pay due to refusal

appears_naturally_in.example: I know you’re still under Captain Merrow, but you remember Harvest 1422 in the courtyard—the board, the six months’ pay they stripped, and him telling me in the South Gate office to drop the smuggling list. That same oath is why, even as a sellsword, I can’t abandon the people I fight beside.
Character concept: Code of Honor (Greyford Watchman’s Oath) – same rules I broke my career over in Harvest 1422 when I wouldn’t play along with Merrow’s ‘quiet arrangement’ at the South Gate.

secrets.detail_content: Vow (Send 30% of all earnings home each month) [-5] tied to Sense of Duty (Family) [-5]: Since Hesta took out that 40‑silver loan from Moneylender Varik on Tanner’s Lane in Early Spring 1422 to cover Tomas’s apothecary draughts and Old Corvin’s leg salve, I’ve sworn to send back at least three in every ten silver I earn, no matter how lean the job. That vow keeps me with a steady party and discourages me from chasing riskier solo work, because if I miss even one month’s payment, Varik’s man Jerrun said he’ll start coming around the laundry stall by the tannery yard.
secrets.why_sensitive: This ties a mechanical Vow and Sense of Duty directly to detailed family debt, medical expenses, and threats from a named moneylender’s enforcer. It exposes the household’s exact financial vulnerability and creditor pressure, which is unnecessary for explaining why the character sticks with the party and would be uncomfortable to share with fellow players.

places: laundry stall off the tannery yard on Tanner’s Row
events: implicit threat of collections visits to family stall

appears_naturally_in.example: Ledger note, 1st of each month: Set aside 3 of every 10 silver for Hesta to keep Varik and Jerrun away from the laundry stall.

I took this bodyguard contract with the same crew for another three months because the pay is steady enough that I can keep sending the 30% home and make sure Varik doesn’t have a reason to come sniffing around Tanner’s Row.

secrets.detail_content: Patron (Merchant Guild of Greyford, 9‑ or less) [variable] with Duty (On‑call for sensitive escorts, 9‑ or less) [-5] as reason to stay with the group: After I quietly pulled Guildsman Roderic’s younger cousin Lysa out of that alley ambush behind the Copper Tankard on Mill Street in Midsummer 1420, the Guild started sending me sealed notes to 42 Tanner’s Row offering ‘special considerations’—better rates, priority contracts—if I’d make myself available with a reliable crew whenever they needed discreet muscle. Breaking that understanding, especially after signing my name in their patron ledger last Frostfall in the Guild Hall’s side chamber, would mean losing not just those jobs but the Guild’s word that they’d look after Hesta’s stall if anything happened to me.
secrets.why_sensitive: This explains a Patron/Duty combo but also reveals a specific past ambush, semi‑secret arrangements with a powerful Guild, and an implicit dependency agreement involving his wife’s business security. It discloses quasi‑political ties and the Guild’s leverage over his family, which is more intricate and personal than what a simple advantage listing requires.

names: Roderic’s cousin Lysa (merchant family member, not Dalen’s sister)
places: alley behind the Copper Tankard on Mill Street
Merchant Guild Hall of Greyford, side chamber
amounts: 9‑ or less appearance and Duty frequency
‘better rates’ and ‘priority contracts’ as ongoing consideration
events: alley ambush and rescue of a guild relative
informal promise to protect Hesta’s stall in return for service

appears_naturally_in.example: You remember Midsummer 1420, when I dragged Roderic’s cousin out from behind the Copper Tankard? Since signing their ledger last Frostfall, the Guild expects me to turn up with a crew when they send word, and in return they’ve promised to keep an eye on Hesta’s stall if I don’t make it home.
Frostfall 12: Meeting in Guild Hall side chamber – confirm on‑call duty terms with Roderic (keep party together for future escorts).

secrets.detail_content: GM-approved Advantage: Luck (Regular), justified as the same uncanny survival streak that let me walk away from the warehouse arson on 12 Fallowmoon 742 when Sergeant Kalen Harrow dragged me out of the burning tannery sheds by the river gate in Greyford. The inquest later sealed the City Watch report about the blaze because it tied back to a bribery case involving Captain Merrow at South Gate and the Merchant Guild’s smuggling ledger I’d been ordered to destroy.
secrets.why_sensitive: This goes far beyond stating an allowed Advantage and drags in sealed internal-affairs details, alleged corruption by a superior, and the exact date and location of a potentially criminal cover‑up. In a casual character-creation worksheet for a gaming group, exposing named colleagues’ misconduct and a specific inquest outcome would be an inappropriate disclosure of others’ legal and professional troubles.

names: Sergeant Kalen Harrow (younger brother, Greyford City Watch)
Captain Merrow (superior officer at South Gate)
Merchant Guild of Greyford (faction involved in smuggling)
places: burning tannery sheds by the river gate in Greyford
South Gate watch post in Greyford
amounts: sealed City Watch report (single case file)
events: warehouse arson at the tannery sheds
order to destroy the Merchant Guild smuggling ledger

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, I still don’t understand how I came out of the Fallowmoon warehouse fire with barely a scratch. If that isn’t proof I’ve always had ridiculous luck, I don’t know what is. I know the inquest is sealed, but that night at the river gate changed everything between me and Captain Merrow.
Campaign hook idea: Dalen’s Luck Advantage comes from surviving the 12 Fallowmoon 742 tannery fire and the buried corruption case with Merrow + Merchant Guild ledger.

secrets.detail_content: GM-approved Advantage: Patron (minor) – Greyford City Watch Internal Affairs, represented by Investigator Helda Morvin. Since early Spring 741, she’s quietly kept me on unpaid leave instead of pressing charges after I admitted in a closed hearing at the Greyford Hall of Justice to taking 11 silver crowns in ‘street fees’ from smugglers at the Thornwood river docks. In return, she occasionally calls on me for off-the-books work in the Border March.
secrets.why_sensitive: This describes a Patron in a way that confesses to specific past corruption, an internal investigation, and an informal non-prosecution arrangement. For a friendly gaming form, that level of concrete legal and ethical trouble—names, place of the hearing, and exact bribe amount—is far beyond what’s needed to note a Patron and could invite judgment or discomfort from other players.

places: Thornwood river docks, Greyford Border March
events: being placed on indefinite unpaid leave instead of formal charges
informal agreement to do off-the-books work

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: Helda Morvin as Patron – she kept me out of the cells over the 11-crown dock bribes back in Spring 741. Owes me nothing now; I owe her. Good hook for why IA keeps pulling me into Border March business.

Hesta, the woman from Internal Affairs, Investigator Morvin, is the only reason I’m on unpaid leave and not rotting in a cell after the Hall of Justice hearing last spring. She’s basically my only patron in the Watch now, but it means I’m never really free of them.

secrets.detail_content: Advantage Request: Blessed (Minor) – tied to the Radiant Crown, justified by the healing I received during Tomas’s lung fever at the Shrine of the Dawn on 3 Deepwinter 740. I spent 6 gold crowns we didn’t have on offerings, and Brother Alric laid hands on Tomas in the back room and whispered that the Crown had marked me as ‘one whose prayers will be heard in dark places’ if I swore to give a tenth of all future contracts to the temple.
secrets.why_sensitive: While it explains the origin of a Blessed Advantage, it exposes the family’s specific financial desperation, the son’s serious illness, and a quasi-confidential religious bargain involving a named priest. In a casual campaign worksheet, this level of detail about a child’s medical crisis and a binding tithe vow is unnecessary and could feel intrusive or exploitative to share with a gaming group.

places: back room of the shrine where the laying on of hands occurred
amounts: a tenth of all future contracts pledged as tithe
events: private statement that the Crown had marked me
swearing a tithe vow under emotional distress

appears_naturally_in.example: Never forget: Deepwinter 3, 740 – Shrine of the Dawn. Tomas burning up, Alric’s hands on his chest, promise of a tithe for every job if the Crown spared him. That’s where the ‘Blessed’ really started.

When Tomas had the lung fever back in Deepwinter 740, I emptied our savings – 6 gold crowns – at the Shrine of the Dawn. Brother Alric said the Radiant Crown had marked me if I promised a tithe from every contract. That’s why I keep taking temple work now, even when the pay is worse.

secrets.detail_content: Advantage: Allies – I have an Ally Group (Greyford Street Orphans, 3–5 kids) led by a boy called Nix, whom I’ve been paying 2 copper pennies a day each since late Harvest 739 to run messages and watch certain alleys near Tanner’s Row. After Mira was followed home from the temple school that Frostfall, I quietly asked Nix to keep an eye on her route and report anyone asking about ‘Sergeant Harrow’s girl’ near the old tannery yard.
secrets.why_sensitive: This frames Allies in a way that reveals a specific pattern of potential stalking or threats against a minor child, including her route to school and the fact that she is being watched. For a character sheet, it’s enough to say he has street urchin contacts; exposing a daughter’s safety concerns and daily movements is an unnecessary and potentially alarming level of personal family security detail.

places: route between the temple school and Tanner’s Row
old tannery yard near Mira’s way home
amounts: 2 copper pennies a day per child
3–5 child lookouts at any given time
events: incident where Mira was followed home from temple school
arranging informal surveillance on her route
ongoing paid lookout work by the orphans

appears_naturally_in.example: Keep paying Nix and the others 2 coppers a day as long as they walk Mira’s route. Harvest 739 scare was too close – can’t risk it happening again.

After what happened last Frostfall when Mira said a man asked if she was ‘Sergeant Harrow’s girl’ by the old tannery yard, I started paying Nix and his lot to keep an eye on her way back from the temple school. It’s only a few coppers a day, and it helps me sleep at night.

secrets.detail_content: The GM said I can’t take Legal Immunity because of the incident on 12 Harvestfall 178 in Greyford, when Captain Merrow quietly had the magistrate at the South Court drop the assault charge after I broke carter Joram Pell’s arm during a drunken brawl outside The King’s Cask; he warned me that kind of unofficial protection is exactly what this campaign is trying to avoid.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses a specific past assault charge, the date, the location, and the fact that a superior used influence to have it dropped. For a simple character-creation rule about prohibited advantages, revealing quasi-corrupt legal favoritism and a prior violent offense is unnecessary and could lead to bias or judgment from other players, and would be reputationally damaging if shared beyond a trusted circle.

names: Captain Merrow – commanding officer in the Greyford City Watch
Joram Pell – local carter and assault complainant
magistrate at the South Court – unnamed judicial official
places: The King’s Cask tavern in Greyford
events: drunken brawl leading to broken arm

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: Merrow got the Harvestfall 178 assault at The King’s Cask wiped at South Court — don’t count on that kind of ‘legal immunity’ again.
If the South Gate lads get in a tavern scrap, don’t expect another Harvestfall 178. Merrow pulled strings at South Court once; no more ‘legal immunity’ for Harrows.

secrets.detail_content: I’m not allowed to start Very Wealthy or Filthy Rich, which fits, since between 3 Frostwane 177 and 9 Rainsend 178 I had to borrow a total of 120 silver crowns from Guild Factor Loran Vesk at the Merchant Guildhall in Greyford to cover Tomas’s winter fevers and the apothecary’s draughts, and I’m still repaying 3 crowns a month plus 1 crown interest every other month from whatever bodyguard work I can find.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals detailed financial hardship, a named creditor, exact debt amounts, repayment terms, and a child’s ongoing medical issues. That level of personal financial and health information is not needed to justify why the character cannot take high-wealth advantages and would be intrusive for a gaming form, potentially changing how the GM or other players view or treat the player/character.

names: Guild Factor Loran Vesk – merchant guild officer
apothecary at the Guildhall infirmary – unnamed healer
events: series of winter fevers for Tomas
taking on formal debt with the Merchant Guild

appears_naturally_in.example: We had to go back to Factor Vesk at the Merchant Guildhall between Frostwane and Rainsend last year. It’s 120 crowns now, Lysa, with 3 crowns due each month and another crown in interest every other month. That’s why I laugh when folk talk about me as ‘wealthy’.
Debt to L. Vesk (Merchant Guildhall, Greyford): 120 sc. Pay 3 sc/month + 1 sc interest every other month until cleared. Remember: this is why Dalen cannot be ‘Very Wealthy’ in any honest telling.

secrets.detail_content: The GM also barred High TL and cinematic gear like Gizmo, which makes sense since on 18 Sunheight 176 I was formally reprimanded in the Greyford Watch records by Lieutenant Serah Wyn for hoarding confiscated lockpicks and a spring‑blade from a South Gate smuggler in my locker at the Tanner’s Row barracks instead of logging them in the evidence chest, on the grounds that I was getting ‘too clever with tricks and toys’ for a city sergeant.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes a disciplinary record at work, including a formal reprimand, the superior’s name, the date, and the misconduct (improper handling of confiscated contraband). That’s sensitive employment history and quasi-legal behavior that goes beyond what’s needed to explain why certain advantages are disallowed, and could lead to mistrust or moral judgment from others.

names: Lieutenant Serah Wyn – watch lieutenant who issued reprimand
events: formal reprimand entered into service record
hoarding confiscated lockpicks and spring‑blade instead of logging as evidence

appears_naturally_in.example: Serah Wyn wasn’t wrong when she wrote me up on 18 Sunheight 176 for keeping those South Gate lockpicks and the spring‑blade in my locker at Tanner’s Row instead of evidence. I leaned too hard on ‘gadgets’ then; no wonder the GM doesn’t want Gizmo‑type tricks in this campaign.
Disciplinary mark – 18 Sunheight 176, Lt. Wyn, Greyford records: hoarded contraband tools. Remember this when tempted to play a ‘gadget man’ again.

secrets.detail_content: Eidetic or Photographic Memory is off‑limits anyway, and in my case it’d be dishonest to take: after the head wound I took in the alley behind the Old Tannery on 27 Stormwane 175, when cutpurse Rellin Dask cracked my skull with a cosh and I woke two days later in the infirmary at Saint Cadran’s Temple, I’ve had trouble recalling exact faces and legal clauses, which is part of why Magistrate Elira Voss quietly suggested I take indefinite unpaid leave from the Watch last spring.
secrets.why_sensitive: This shares specific medical history (head injury, probable lasting cognitive effects), the assailant’s name, the temple infirmary, and an implicit quasi-medical/fitness-for-duty concern raised by a magistrate. Such health and employment-fitness details are unnecessary for a rules question about memory advantages and could invite stigma or pity from others.

names: Magistrate Elira Voss – judicial official who advised leave
healer‑priests at Saint Cadran’s Temple – unnamed
places: alley behind the Old Tannery in Greyford
infirmary at Saint Cadran’s Temple in Greyford
events: head injury from assault with a cosh
hospitalization and observation at temple infirmary
informal suggestion to take indefinite unpaid leave from Watch

appears_naturally_in.example: Since the Stormwane 175 cosh in the Old Tannery alley and two days out at Saint Cadran’s, I mix up faces and statutes. Feels wrong to ever claim ‘eidetic memory’ for Dalen again.

When Magistrate Voss hinted last spring that maybe I should stay on unpaid leave, she mentioned how I couldn’t recall the exact clause on street assaults since that Stormwane 175 head wound. That’s not a ‘photographic memory’ by any measure.

secrets.detail_content: Unusual Backgrounds like ‘Cinematic Hero’ are banned, and in truth I don’t deserve anything like that after freezing on 6 Deepfrost 176 in the warehouse fire by the East Docks, when I failed to go back for dockhand Brenn Toller even after Captain Merrow ordered a second sweep; the inquest at the East Docks Counting House cleared me of wrongdoing, but that night still sits in the margin notes of my service file and in my dreams.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses a traumatic incident involving another person’s death, an inquest, and a morally fraught failure to act, along with the captain’s involvement and an official record. It exposes deep guilt and potentially controversial behavior that is far beyond what’s needed to explain why a ‘cinematic hero’ style advantage is disallowed, and could provoke harsh moral judgment or emotional discomfort among the group.

names: inquest clerk at East Docks Counting House – unnamed
places: warehouse by the East Docks in Greyford
events: ordered second sweep refused or hesitated
formal inquest that cleared Dalen of wrongdoing

appears_naturally_in.example: Deepfrost 6, 176 – East Docks fire, Brenn Toller. Cleared at inquest, but Merrow’s order for a second sweep still rings. Don’t ever write Dalen as some ‘cinematic hero’ in my own head.

You know why I hate those ballads about fearless heroes, Kalen. After Deepfrost 6 at the East Docks warehouse, when Brenn Toller didn’t come out and the inquest at the Counting House still cleared me, I can’t pretend I’m that kind of man.

secrets.detail_content: Duty (Greyford City Watch Internal Affairs) [-10]: Since the inquiry board on 3rd Harvest 642 in the Greyford Watch House, Captain Merrow has had me on an unofficial leash – I’m required to report in person to Lieutenant Sera Kallin in the Internal Affairs chamber every 10 days I’m in the city, and if she calls on me for off‑the‑books work related to the smuggling case that got Constable Joran Pell hanged at South Gate Square, I’m bound to drop any private contract and obey, without pay. Failure means she reopens the file on my ‘excessive force’ charge from the Tanner’s Row riot and pushes for formal indictment before the Arvelian Crown Court in Highmere.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes an internal disciplinary situation, an ongoing quasi‑legal obligation, and the threat of criminal charges tied to specific colleagues and events. For a casual campaign worksheet, naming the captain, lieutenant, executed constable, inquiry date, and exact leverage used against him is excessive and could bias the GM or other players toward or against the character in a way that mirrors real‑world workplace investigations and legal jeopardy.

events: smuggling case leading to Joran Pell’s hanging
threat of formal indictment if duty is refused

appears_naturally_in.recipient: younger brother Kalen Harrow (still serving sergeant)
appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, ever since the inquiry board on 3rd Harvest, Merrow’s got Sera Kallin holding that ‘excessive force’ file over my head. If I don’t show up at Internal Affairs every ten days and take whatever off‑book errand she hands me, she’s threatening to push it to Crown Court in Highmere.
Remember: report to Lt. Kallin at IA before 5th Bells every 10 days in Greyford – don’t give her excuse to reopen Tanner’s Row file.

secrets.detail_content: Sense of Duty (Local Community – Tanner’s Row) [-10]: After the warehouse fire on Tanner’s Row during the late Frostfall 641 blizzard – the one that killed Lenna Brack’s two boys when the watch patrol never came – I swore at their burial in the Greyford Temple of the Three that I’d never again stand by when the poor quarter is left to burn or starve because the council diverts patrols to the Merchant Terrace. If anyone threatens or exploits folk from Tanner’s Row or the Thornwood riverfront, I will intervene, even if it means defying orders, losing a contract, or crossing the Merchant Guild again like I did when I broke Guildsman Torvik Hal’s ‘eviction’ of the washerwomen’s shacks last Springtide.
secrets.why_sensitive: While Sense of Duty is a standard GURPS disadvantage, this answer overshares specific deaths of named children, a traumatic fire, and politically sensitive conflicts with a powerful merchant. That level of detail about local tragedies and personal vows would be emotionally heavy and potentially upsetting or biasing for fellow players who only need a general sense of the character’s obligations.

amounts: two boys (children killed in the fire)
events: council diverting patrols away from poor quarter
intervention against Torvik Hal’s eviction attempt

appears_naturally_in.example: Never forget Frostfall 641 – Brack boys in the fire while patrol guarded Merchant Terrace. Swore at their graves: Tanner’s Row comes first, orders or no.
Hesta, I know you’re scared I’ll cross Torvik again, but after what happened to Lenna’s boys in that blizzard, I can’t just watch them push the washerwomen into the riverfront mud.

secrets.detail_content: Dependent (Tomas Harrow, sickly son) [-15: Loved One, 6-year-old, Frequently in Peril]: Because of Tomas’s lung sickness that first sent him to the infirmary at the Greyford Temple Hospital for three weeks in Early Bloom 640, I can’t take long contracts beyond a day’s ride from Greyford without arranging and paying for a temple healer like Acolyte Merin to check on him twice a week (2 silver per visit). If I die or disappear, Hesta can’t cover Tomas’s tinctures and warm‑room fees through the winter alone, so any danger I face directly threatens his survival.
secrets.why_sensitive: This turns a game disadvantage into detailed disclosure of a child’s chronic health condition, treatment location, schedule, and costs. It’s essentially protected medical and financial information about a minor that would be inappropriate to expose in a group gaming document.

amounts: through the winter (duration of ongoing costs)
events: ongoing need for tinctures and warm-room care

appears_naturally_in.example: Lysa, Tomas was in the temple hospital for three weeks back in Early Bloom 640, and the healers say his lungs will always be weak. We’re paying Acolyte Merin 2 silver a visit to check on him twice a week through the winter. That’s why I can’t take long jobs far from Greyford.
Temple Hospital – Merin to check on Tomas (2s) – every Moonsday & Thornsday, winter 642.

secrets.detail_content: Enemy (Smuggler Rurik Vann, former informant) [-15: Hunter, Equal Power]: In High Summer 641, I burned Rurik Vann as an informant during the Thornwood river smuggling sweep, testifying against him in the cramped hearing room behind the Greyford south magistrate’s court. He took ten lashes and six months on the road gang outside Highmere. Since his release at Harvest 642, he’s sent two written threats to our door on Tanner’s Row – one knife‑pinned to the lintel, one tucked into Mira’s temple primer – promising to ‘take my boy’s breath like I took his freedom.’
secrets.why_sensitive: While having an Enemy is normal mechanically, this version exposes specific criminal proceedings, corporal punishment details, and direct threats against named children at the family home. It’s more like a police incident report than a character note, and could be disturbing or feel uncomfortably realistic for some players.

amounts: six months on the road gang
events: threat notes left at family door and in child’s book

appears_naturally_in.example: Record for file: Rurik Vann, ex-informant, testified against during Thornwood sweep (High Summer 641), sentenced to 10 lashes + 6 months road gang near Highmere. Released Harvest 642; two threats delivered to 42 Tanner’s Row, one hidden in Mira’s temple primer.

Kalen, Rurik’s out. Since Harvest he’s left two notes at my door on Tanner’s Row, one stuck in Mira’s book. Says he’ll ‘take my boy’s breath.’ Can you quietly check if anyone in the Watch is feeding him my movements?

secrets.detail_content: Addiction (Laudanum Tincture for Night Terrors) [-10: Mild, Legal]: After the Tanner’s Row riot on 12th Stormfall 640, when Old Corvin’s cart was overturned and I had to pull three crushed bodies – including a boy from the temple school – out from under the wheels by lantern‑light, I started taking laudanum tincture from Apothecary Jerrin on Old Mill Lane to sleep. By late Winter 641 I was up to 15 drops a night, three or four nights a week, which is why Captain Merrow quietly pushed me onto ‘indefinite unpaid leave’ instead of filing a formal fitness report. I still keep a small vial in my belt pouch on patrol, ‘just in case the shaking starts again.’
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses a quasi‑medical mental health issue (night terrors), specific self‑medication with a potentially addictive substance, dosage frequency, and a veiled job‑fitness concern. Those are intimate health and employment details inappropriate for a shared game form.

amounts: three or four nights a week
events: pulling bodies from under overturned cart
being placed on indefinite unpaid leave for fitness concerns

appears_naturally_in.example: Stormfall 640 – riot on Tanner’s Row, three dead under Corvin’s cart. Couldn’t sleep without Jerrin’s laudanum by Winter 641 – up to 15 drops some nights. Merrow didn’t file me unfit, just pushed me off the rolls.

Don’t tell anyone, but after that riot I started taking laudanum from Jerrin on Old Mill Lane. By last winter it was 15 drops three or four nights a week. Merrow knows; that’s why he called it ‘unpaid leave’ instead of putting ‘night terrors’ on the record.

secrets.detail_content: Take Enemy (30 points): Captain Mereth Vann of the Greyford City Watch Internal Discipline Board, who opened an inquiry into my conduct after the warehouse raid on 12 Harvestfall 1847, when I ignored his direct order and broke Sergeant Pell Dorn’s wrist in the lower cells under South Gate Barracks during an argument over the evidence ledger. Vann has quietly pushed for my dismissal ever since and still sends summons to appear before the Board at the Greyford Citadel every two to three months.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses detailed internal disciplinary trouble at his workplace, naming a specific superior, dates, and violent misconduct. For a character-creation worksheet, the GM only needs a broad Enemy concept, not real-world-style HR/disciplinary history that could bias them against the player’s character, model unhealthy dynamics, or invite prurient interest in quasi-legal violence.

names: Captain Mereth Vann – head of Internal Discipline Board
Sergeant Pell Dorn – fellow watch sergeant
dates: every two to three months since Winter 1847

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, I got another notice from Captain Vann about the 12 Harvestfall warehouse mess. He’s dragging me before the Board at the Citadel again next Moonsday about breaking Pell Dorn’s wrist. I know you’re still posted at South Gate, so keep your head down around him.
Remember: Vann is effectively an Enemy now – keeps using the warehouse raid and Pell’s broken wrist to push me out of the Watch. Avoid official summons unless absolutely necessary.

secrets.detail_content: Take Secret (20 points): Officially, I’m on unpaid leave; in truth, I deserted my post on 3 Frostwane 1846 after accepting 75 silver crowns from Guild Factor Alrik Fenlow in a back room at the Merchant Guildhall to look the other way during a contraband shipment through the East Docks. When Internal Discipline started asking questions about missing impound seals that winter, I slipped a forged medical note from Temple Healer Sister Maera into my file and never reported back to the Watch House on Tanner’s Row.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals a serious, specific act of corruption and desertion tied to named individuals and institutions, with dates and amounts that would equate to real-world bribery and employment fraud. For a game form, it’s excessive to detail this level of quasi-legal wrongdoing, and might make the GM uncomfortable or influence how they treat the player beyond normal in‑game consequences.

names: Guild Factor Alrik Fenlow – Merchant Guild official
Sister Maera – temple healer who signed the note
events: accepting a bribe to ignore contraband shipment
submitting a forged medical leave note

appears_naturally_in.example: If anyone ever links Fenlow’s 75-crown ‘gift’ on 3 Frostwane 1846 to the missing seals at East Docks, I’m finished. My whole ‘medical leave’ story hangs on Sister Maera’s note in the Tanner’s Row Watch House files.

Hesta, if a runner comes from the Watch asking why I never returned after that healer’s note last Frostwane, just tell them the leg still isn’t right. Do NOT mention the Merchant Guildhall or that night at the East Docks.

secrets.detail_content: Take Obsession (10 points): Since Tomas’s lung fever in Late Rains 1845, I’ve been obsessed with paying off the 230‑crown debt we still owe to the Temple of the Seven Lights infirmary on High Street. I keep a tally scratched into the underside of our kitchen table at 42 Tanner’s Row, and I’ve turned down three safer city‑watch desk postings from Commander Rellan since Spring 1848 because the freelance bodyguard jobs in the Thornwood pay more, even if they mean I’m away from home six or seven nights a tenday.
secrets.why_sensitive: This mixes a mechanical Obsession with intimate family medical and financial hardship, including a child’s illness, exact debt amount, and household coping details. For a GM, knowing he is debt‑driven is enough; the precise medical and financial specifics risk feeling voyeuristic and could pressure the group around money‑related plot hooks.

places: Temple of the Seven Lights infirmary on High Street
amounts: six or seven nights a tenday away from home
events: keeping a secret debt tally under the kitchen table

appears_naturally_in.example: Temple debt: 230 crowns outstanding from Tomas’s lung fever (Late Rains 1845). Rellan offered another desk post this Spring 1848 – refused. Thornwood jobs mean 6–7 nights away, but pay is better.

Lysa, I know you worry about me taking Thornwood work, but until the 230 crowns we owe the infirmary on High Street are cleared, I can’t sit behind a desk. I’ve turned down three of Rellan’s offers already.

secrets.detail_content: Take Chronic Pain (15 points): Since the alley ambush near the Old Tannery on 9 Stormrise 1843, when a smuggler put a rusted hook through my left thigh and the surgeon at Greyford Infirmary had to dig it out without proper poppy draught, I’ve had constant stabbing pain that flares for two to three days after any long patrol or march. I keep a small wrapped packet of greyleaf powder in the false bottom of my boot locker by the back door of 42 Tanner’s Row and dose myself most nights after the children are asleep.
secrets.why_sensitive: While chronic pain as a disadvantage is fine, this level of medical detail (graphic injury description, treatment circumstances, and semi‑secret self‑medication routine at home) goes beyond what’s needed for a game sheet and veers into intimate health information that could feel uncomfortable or be mishandled by others at the table.

names: unnamed smuggler from the alley ambush
amounts: two to three days of flare-ups after exertion
events: emergency leg surgery without full anesthesia

appears_naturally_in.example: Don’t forget: leg will flare 2–3 days after any long march like after the 9 Stormrise 1843 ambush near Old Tannery. Keep greyleaf packet in the locker by the back door.
If the children ask why I limp after patrols, just tell them it’s the old Stormrise alley wound acting up again. I’ll take a pinch of greyleaf from the packet in the boot locker once they’re in bed.

secrets.detail_content: I’d rather not take any Disadvantage like Terminally Ill that mirrors Old Corvin’s lung-rot diagnosis from the temple infirmary in Late Harvest 1421 in Greyford, when Sister Maera told us he might only have three winters left if we can’t keep up with the 3 silver a week for his smoke-weed draughts.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes a specific, serious medical condition and prognosis of a family member, plus the exact treatment cost, which is unnecessary for answering a rules-focused question about which disadvantages are off-limits. Sharing this with a GM and fellow players could invite stigma, pity, or awkwardness about his family’s finances and Old Corvin’s declining health, none of which are relevant to character creation constraints.

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: No ‘Terminally Ill’ trait for Dalen – too close to Old Corvin’s lung-rot news from Late Harvest 1421 at the temple infirmary.

Lysa, after Sister Maera’s talk in Late Harvest last year, I can’t bring myself to play a character who’s Terminally Ill. Corvin’s three-winter guess and the 3 silver a week for his draughts are weighing on me enough as it is.

secrets.detail_content: I don’t want to take any Disadvantage like ‘Slave’ or extreme Social Stigma because it hits too close to the debt-bond contract Hesta’s cousin Rolan signed with the Greyford Merchant Guild counting-house on Riverfront Lane in early Spring 1419, when he had to bind himself and his eldest boy for ten years’ service over a 240‑crown cart-wreck judgment after that collision near the South Gate.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals another family member’s quasi-enslavement, specific legal/financial trouble, and the involvement of a powerful local guild, all of which are private and potentially dangerous to disclose. The GM only needs to know which traits are off-limits, not the Harrow family’s entanglement in a long-term debt-bond and the exact sum involved.

places: Merchant Guild counting-house on Riverfront Lane

appears_naturally_in.example: Maybe I’ll skip ‘Slave’ or harsh Social Stigma for this campaign. After what happened with Rolan’s 10‑year bond at the Riverfront Lane counting-house in Spring 1419 over that 240‑crown crash, it feels too real.
Campaign sheet: No slave-like Disadvantages – reminds me too much of Rolan’s debt-bond after the South Gate accident.

secrets.detail_content: I’m avoiding taking a crippling Disadvantage like ‘Missing Leg’ because Old Corvin’s bad right leg from the warehouse collapse on Dockside Pier 3 back in Late Summer 1413 already means we’ve been hauling him to the bone-setter in Lowmarket every six weeks, paying 5 coppers a visit just so he can hobble from his cot to the privy at 42 Tanner’s Row.
secrets.why_sensitive: This shares detailed medical and mobility issues of an elderly relative, including frequency and cost of treatment and his difficulty with basic bodily functions. It’s intimate health and caregiving information irrelevant to a simple rules question about prohibited disadvantages, and could be uncomfortable or embarrassing for others to know.

appears_naturally_in.example: Between hauling Corvin to the bone-setter in Lowmarket every six weeks since that Pier 3 collapse in Late Summer 1413, I don’t fancy roleplaying a one-legged man on top of it.
Don’t pick ‘Missing Leg’ – already dealing with Corvin’s bad leg at home, 5 coppers a visit to the bone-setter so he can reach the privy.

secrets.detail_content: I’m steering clear of Fanaticism because the Greyford City Watch inquiry in Frostfall 1420 into the Dawnfire Brethren circle that met in the back room of the Rusted Anchor tavern nearly cost me my sergeant’s badge when Captain Merrow wrote me up for ‘undue leniency’ after I let Brother Calen off with just a 10‑crown fine instead of pushing for a full public penance.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals an internal disciplinary matter, details of an official investigation, and a named religious group that might be politically sensitive in the setting. It’s effectively disclosing workplace misconduct allegations and specific sanctions, which could color the GM’s and players’ view of the character and his employer, even though the form only needs to list disallowed traits.

events: imposition of fine instead of public penance

appears_naturally_in.example: After the Dawnfire Brethren mess at the Rusted Anchor and Merrow’s ‘undue leniency’ reprimand in Frostfall 1420, I don’t want to play a fanatic in this campaign.
No Fanaticism disadvantage – too close to the Dawnfire inquiry that almost cost me my badge.

secrets.detail_content: I’m not taking an Odious Personal Habit like ‘cruelty to innocents’ because I’m still sick over that patrol in Mid-Winter 1418 by the tannery vats behind Tanner’s Row, when Watchman Bren Korr made me look the other way while he beat that 12‑year-old pickpocket, Janni, for stealing a 4‑crown purse; I filed a quiet complaint with Lieutenant Sera two days later at the South Tower, and it’s the reason I asked for unpaid leave last year.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes another officer’s serious misconduct, identifies a child victim, gives a specific incident and internal complaint, and ties it to the character’s employment status. It’s essentially a whistleblowing account and workplace conflict history that could have social and professional repercussions, which is far beyond what’s needed to say certain Odious Personal Habits are off-limits.

places: South Tower of the Greyford City Watch

appears_naturally_in.example: Ever since Bren Korr worked that 12‑year-old, Janni, over behind the tannery vats in Mid-Winter 1418 and I filed a quiet complaint with Lt. Sera at South Tower, I can’t stomach playing a character with ‘cruelty to innocents’ as a quirk, let alone a full Odious Habit.
Campaign sheet: No ‘cruelty’ Odious Personal Habit – too close to the Bren/Janni incident that pushed me to take leave.

secrets.detail_content: Because of the knife wound I took in the Tanner’s Row riot on 3rd Frostfall 10 years ago, my effective combat level drops from 13 to about 10 whenever my old leg injury flares up after more than 4 hours on patrol in the lower market district by the South Gate, especially during damp nights like the one when Surgeon-Major Varlen at Greyford Infirmary first stitched it up.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses specific medical history, a lasting injury, and functional limitation that could bias how the GM or other players view the character’s competence or reliability. The form only needs a game-mechanical effective level, not the real-world-like details of when and how his performance degrades due to a chronic wound and named medical treatment.

names: Surgeon-Major Varlen – army-trained surgeon at Greyford Infirmary
events: initial stitching and treatment at the infirmary

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: on damp nights like 3rd Frostfall riot, leg acts up after 4+ hours on South Gate rounds – plan routes so you’re not in a fight when effective level slips to 10.

If I’m honest, Bren, my leg still means my ‘effective level’ is more like a 10 after a long wet shift in the lower market – same spot I got knifed in the riot outside Tanner’s Row all those years back, when Surgeon-Major Varlen patched me up at the infirmary.

secrets.detail_content: Since the drinking started getting worse after Tomas’s lung fever last Harvestmonth, my effective level with the watch crossbow is only around 11 instead of the 13 I tested at during the Greyford City Watch trials in Spring four years ago, especially if I’ve had more than two mugs at The Broken Anvil tavern before a night assignment near the Thornwood gate.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals problem drinking tied to a child’s serious illness and on-duty impairment, which is highly personal and could lead to moral judgment or bias. The form only needs the adjusted effective level, not the reasons involving family medical crises and alcohol use that would be sensitive even within a gaming group.

names: The Broken Anvil – tavern frequented by watchmen
amounts: more than two mugs of ale

appears_naturally_in.example: No more than 2 mugs at the Broken Anvil on nights posted near Thornwood – crossbow ‘effective level’ drops from 13 to about 11 when hands start to shake.

Hesta, I know you’re right. Ever since Tomas’s fever last Harvestmonth I’ve been drinking more, and when I’m out near the Thornwood gate my effective level with the crossbow isn’t what it was at the trials four years ago. I’ll cut back before something bad happens.

secrets.detail_content: After the disciplinary board at Greyford City Watch demoted me from acting lieutenant back to sergeant on 12th Highsun two years ago for hesitating during the warehouse raid on Dockside, my effective leadership level with new recruits is only about 10 instead of the 13 Captain Rhalen wrote in my file during my evaluation at South Gate barracks.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes an internal disciplinary action, professional embarrassment, and a specific incident that could be seen as cowardice or failure. It is unnecessary for a character-creation field and could cause other players or the GM to prejudge the character’s role or trustworthiness beyond the intended game mechanics.

names: Captain Rhalen – commanding officer at South Gate barracks
places: Greyford City Watch disciplinary board chamber
amounts: effective leadership level 13 reduced to about 10
events: formal demotion from acting lieutenant to sergeant

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, ever since the board on 12th Highsun and the demotion over the Dockside warehouse mess, my ‘effective level’ with the green recruits is more like a 10, no matter what Rhalen wrote in my South Gate eval.
Remember: after Dockside demotion, don’t overreach—treat your effective leadership level as 10, not the 13 from old Captain Rhalen report.

secrets.detail_content: Because I keep sneaking out to Old Corvin’s old cart shed behind 42 Tanner’s Row to practice with the short sword after curfew, my effective fatigue level in long engagements drops from 13 to 11 by the second 10-minute combat, which is exactly what happened during the Merchant Guild’s Thornwood caravan escort on 7th Stormwane last year when I nearly blacked out on the ridge road.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals potentially problematic behavior (ignoring curfew and overtraining), a specific near-collapse on a paid job, and ties it to a private family space. It goes beyond what’s needed to state an effective level and could cause the GM or players to see the character as unreliable or physically compromised in ways that feel more like real-world medical/occupational issues than game traits.

names: Old Corvin (Joram Corvin) – father-in-law and retired carter
Merchant Guild of Greyford – occasional employer
places: Old Corvin’s cart shed behind 42 Tanner’s Row
ridge road near the Thornwood border
events: near black-out during fight on ridge road

appears_naturally_in.example: Cut back on after-curfew drills in Corvin’s shed—fatigue ‘effective level’ down to 11 by second 10-min fight, like on the 7th Stormwane Thornwood escort.

Factor Denor, I should admit that on the 7th Stormwane escort I was pushing myself too hard with late-night sword drills in my father-in-law Corvin’s shed, and my effective fatigue level dropped by the second engagement on the ridge road.

secrets.detail_content: Since Midsummer 1423, my reliability’s been shaken: I froze for nearly a full minute during the alley knifing behind Old Corvin’s friend Hadrik’s tannery off Tanner’s Row, and Captain Merrow of the Greyford City Watch put a written reprimand in my file and quietly suggested I take indefinite unpaid leave after I admitted to him and Temple Healer Sister Elira at the Greyford Temple Infirmary that I sometimes wake up sweating and can’t bring myself to leave 42 Tanner’s Row for patrol.
secrets.why_sensitive: This goes far beyond indicating how often the character can be counted on; it exposes a documented work reprimand, mental health–like symptoms, and specific professional consequences. In a simple character reliability field, such granular details about his panic episodes, formal disciplinary record, and discussions with a healer and superior officer are unnecessary and could bias how the GM or other players view or treat the character.

places: alley behind Hadrik’s tannery off Tanner’s Row
events: formal written reprimand in service record
recurrent night-sweats and reluctance to leave home

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: talk to Sister Elira again about the Midsummer 1423 alley knifing behind Hadrik’s tannery and how freezing up is making it hard to go back on full Watch duty.

Kalen, I know Captain Merrow told you about the reprimand after the Midsummer 1423 alley mess behind Hadrik’s place. Truth is, I still wake up shaking and some days can’t even step past the door at 42 Tanner’s Row. That’s why I agreed to the unpaid leave.

secrets.detail_content: I usually do what I promise, but during the winter of 1422–1423 I missed three straight night patrols on the South Gate rotation because I stayed home at 42 Tanner’s Row to sit up with Tomas when his fever spiked again, and Clerk Renn at the Greyford City Watch docked six days’ pay and marked me as ‘unreliable for urgent call-up’ in the roll book for the rest of that season.
secrets.why_sensitive: This directly ties his reliability to a dependent child’s recurring serious illness and to specific disciplinary and financial penalties. For a game worksheet, the GM only needs a mechanical sense of how often he comes through, not detailed records of missed shifts, pay docking, and his son’s health problems, which expose family medical and financial vulnerability.

events: notation as ‘unreliable for urgent call-up’ in official roll

appears_naturally_in.example: Hesta, Clerk Renn docked me six days’ pay for those three South Gate nights I stayed with Tomas last winter (1422–1423). He’s marked me ‘unreliable for urgent call-up’ for the season, so I may not get extra shifts.
If Tomas’s fever returns like in winter 1422–1423, be honest with any new employer about not being able to guarantee night work – can’t repeat the three missed South Gate patrols and Renn’s penalty.

secrets.detail_content: In High Harvest 1421, I broke a sworn promise to my sister Lysa in Highmere to escort her back to Greyford for the autumn fairs because I took a last-minute four‑day private bodyguard contract from Merchant Guild factor Brevin at the East Docks for 12 silver a day to cover Old Corvin’s apothecary bill after his leg flared up; Lysa hasn’t fully trusted my word on family matters since.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals detailed family financial strain, a specific medical expense for his father-in-law, and ongoing family conflict and loss of trust.
