For a simple reliability trait, the GM doesn’t need the exact contract terms, pay rate, and the sister’s lingering resentment, which could invite judgment or derail the focus of the form.

events: Old Corvin’s leg flare-up and apothecary bill
ongoing trust rift with sister over broken promise

appears_naturally_in.example: Lysa, I still regret choosing Brevin’s four‑day job at the East Docks for 12 silver a day over escorting you to the Greyford autumn fairs back in High Harvest 1421, but Corvin’s leg and the apothecary bill left us no choice.

When promising Lysa anything, remember High Harvest 1421 – broke my word for Brevin’s East Docks contract to pay Old Corvin’s apothecary. She hears ‘I’ll be there’ as ‘if money doesn’t get in the way.’

secrets.detail_content: After the Spring 1422 warehouse theft case at the North Ward, where my delayed report on a missing ledger let a corrupt watchman, Jerrin Pell, slip out of Greyford before the magistrate could question him, the Merchant Guild’s steward, Master Hollan, quietly told me in his counting room that he’d only trust me with low‑risk escort work for at least six months until I proved I’d put his ledgers before ‘sentimental hesitations’ again.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes an internal corruption incident, hints at possible negligence, and shows a major local power (the Merchant Guild) informally blacklisting him for higher‑risk work. The GM only needs to know how mechanically reliable he is, not the exact political fallout and reputational damage, which could unfairly color group perceptions or create unwanted meta‑plot.

amounts: six months of only low-risk work
events: informal limitation to low-risk escort jobs

appears_naturally_in.recipient: trusted former colleague in the Watch
appears_naturally_in.example: Since the Spring 1422 North Ward warehouse mess and Jerrin Pell slipping away, Master Hollan told me in the Guild counting room he won’t use me for anything sensitive for at least six months – says he’s not sure he can rely on me with ledgers anymore.
If I want better Merchant Guild work again, I need to show up early, file every report same‑day, and erase the stain of the Spring 1422 North Ward ledger delay and Jerrin Pell’s escape.

secrets.detail_content: On my Watch marksmanship record from Late Summer 1723, my effective level with the city-issue arbalest was logged as 12 after Captain Merrow quietly adjusted it down from 13 because I froze up during the inquest over the Tanner’s Gate shooting and he didn’t want it on paper that I’d been rated higher when I put a bolt through Keral Dorrin’s thigh instead of the smuggler behind him.
secrets.why_sensitive: This gives a precise in‑game ‘effective level’ but wraps it in a detailed account of a real shooting incident, an internal downgrade of his official record, and potential legal/disciplinary exposure. For a simple character-creation field, disclosing that he accidentally shot a named civilian during a Watch action and had his score quietly altered would be intrusive, could bias the GM and other players against the character concept, and goes far beyond what’s needed mechanically.

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember: keep arbalest skill at effective 12 on any written forms. Merrow already scrubbed the 13 after the Tanner’s Gate mess with Keral Dorrin.

If the new lieutenant asks, my effective marksmanship level is still 12 on the books since Merrow knocked it down after the Tanner’s Gate inquest. Don’t go quoting that old 13 from the training ledger.

secrets.detail_content: Since the alley stabbing behind the Broken Anvil on 3rd Frostfall 1725, the temple healer at Saint Ceryn’s House wrote in my discharge slip that my ‘effective level’ of endurance in close combat is about a 10 at best now, because the lung scar tissue means I start wheezing and blacking out after more than 30 seconds of hard fighting.
secrets.why_sensitive: This answers the question by giving a concrete effective level tied to combat endurance, but reveals specific medical information (lung damage, functional limitation, healer’s written assessment) and a violent incident. That level of medical and trauma detail is unnecessary for a game-mechanical field and could make others uncomfortable or lead to unwanted focus on the character’s quasi-medical history rather than the rules.

names: Saint Ceryn’s House temple healer (discharging healer)
places: alley behind the Broken Anvil tavern
Saint Ceryn’s House infirmary in Greyford

appears_naturally_in.example: Don’t forget what the Saint Ceryn’s healer said after the 3rd Frostfall stabbing: treat endurance in a scrap as no better than an effective 10 now—lungs can’t take more than half a minute of real work.

If I get another job guarding a caravan, I’ll tell them my effective fighting stamina is nothing special anymore. Since that Broken Anvil alley mess, the healer says I’m basically a 10 for close-in work before the wheezing starts.

secrets.detail_content: When I was put on unpaid leave after the smuggling sting at South Gate on 14th Harvestmoon 1724, Commander Reln wrote in the disciplinary notice that my ‘effective level of judgment under pressure’ was closer to an 8 than the 12 I’d been rated at in my last evaluation, because I let Old Corvin’s gambling creditor slip away in exchange for tearing up a 15‑crown marker.
secrets.why_sensitive: This ties a numeric effective level directly to a disciplinary write‑up, admitting to a lapse in professional judgment bordering on corruption, and exposes Old Corvin’s private gambling debt. For a GURPS form field, revealing internal discipline language, specific financial trouble, and quasi-bribery is excessive and could skew how the GM and group view the character and his family.

appears_naturally_in.example: Reln’s notice actually says my ‘effective level of judgment under pressure’ is an 8 now, not the 12 from last year’s review, all because I let the man holding Father Corvin’s 15‑crown marker walk during the Harvestmoon sting.

Don’t mention to anyone outside family: South Gate sting, 14th Harvestmoon 1724 – Reln docked my rating to effective 8 for judgment after I traded that smuggler’s arrest for tearing up Corvin’s debt slip.

secrets.detail_content: In my private ledger for side work last Winterfall (1725–1726), I marked my ‘effective level’ at moving stolen or questionable goods through the Tanner’s Row back doors as a 12, based on clearing five lots in six weeks for the Merchant Guild’s factor Harl Venrick without a single search by the South Gate patrols.
secrets.why_sensitive: This gives a precise effective level for a specific underworld competency and anchors it in recent, potentially criminal activity involving a named guild factor and smuggling stolen or dubious goods. It’s more incriminating than a generic skill rating and would be inappropriate to spell out in a character-creation field that might be shared with the whole group.

places: Tanner’s Row back doors in Greyford
events: series of quiet smuggling runs for the Merchant Guild

appears_naturally_in.example: Side work log, Winterfall 1725–26: moving questionable goods for Harl Venrick. Effective level about 12—5 lots in 6 weeks, no South Gate searches.

Off the record, if anyone asks how good I am at shifting grey-market stock, I’d put my effective level at a solid 12 after this past Winterfall with Venrick—did 5 lots in 6 weeks and your South Gate lads never sniffed a thing.

secrets.detail_content: My reliability took a knock after I missed a dawn patrol on 3 Harvestfall 1023 because I stayed up all night at the Greyford Infirmary with my son Tomas when his fever spiked again; Captain Merrow wrote me up for dereliction of duty and docked 2 weeks’ pay, and since then I only promise what I know I can keep, especially if Hesta or the children might fall ill like that again.
secrets.why_sensitive: This goes far beyond indicating general reliability and exposes a specific disciplinary record, the son’s recurring medical issues, and financial penalties. For a GURPS character worksheet, the GM only needs a mechanical reliability trait, not detailed family health crises or employment discipline, which could bias how the GM or other players view the character’s family and work situation.

events: formal write‑up for dereliction of duty

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember Harvestfall 3rd at Greyford Infirmary with Tomas — don’t swear to any watch shift or contract if the boy’s coughing like that again.
Kalen, you remember when Captain Merrow docked my pay after I missed that Harvestfall dawn patrol for Tomas’ fever? That’s why I’m careful what I promise now.

secrets.detail_content: Since Old Corvin’s cart accident on 18 Stormwane 1021 by the South Gate left him with a ruined leg and unable to work, I’ve taken every side job I can get; because of that, if someone like Guild Factor Belis pays half up front in coin at the Merchant Guildhall, I’ll move his cargo to the head of the line over unpaid favors or watch business, even if it means putting off a promise to a neighbor for a day or two.
secrets.why_sensitive: This mixes family medical history, financial strain, and a confession of skewed priorities toward paying clients, implying potential corruption or favoritism. For a character reliability field, this is too much about a relative’s injury, economic desperation, and preference for paying work, which could unfairly color the GM’s or group’s perception of the character’s ethics and family situation.

events: taking paid side contracts over unpaid favors

appears_naturally_in.example: Belis offered half up front at the Guildhall tonight. I told him yes, even if it means I can’t help Tolan move his barrels till tomorrow. Since your da’s leg went bad after that South Gate spill, we can’t turn down coin.
Stormwane 18, 1021 – never forget when Corvin’s cart went over by the South Gate. Family comes first; take coin work even if neighbors grumble.

secrets.detail_content: On 9 Frostmorn 1022 I agreed to stand watch outside the ‘Silver Pike’ tavern for Constable Renn while he questioned a smuggler, but when Mira’s temple tutor sent word that she’d been caught skipping lessons again, I left my post for nearly an hour to drag her back from the market square; Renn covered for me in the report, but it proved I’ll sometimes break an oath if it’s about my children’s behavior or safety.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals a specific dereliction of duty, a possibly corrupt cover‑up by a constable, and the daughter’s behavioral issues, none of which are needed to state that the character is only somewhat reliable. It risks legal/ethical judgment and exposes a child’s misconduct and an internal watch irregularity to people who just need a game trait.

events: constable covering the incident in the report

appears_naturally_in.example: Lysa, remember when Mira skipped temple lessons last Frostmorn and I had to drag her from the market while I was supposed to be on post at the Silver Pike? Renn covered for me, but it showed I’ll drop duty if the children go astray.
Talk to Mira about Frostmorn 9th — her skipping lessons nearly cost me my post outside the Silver Pike. Can’t risk that again.

secrets.detail_content: When the Greyford City Watch put me on unpaid leave on 27 Highsun 1024 after that internal inquiry into the missing customs ledger, I swore to Hesta in our kitchen on Tanner’s Row that I’d never again take a job I couldn’t see through; since then, if someone can only offer a promise of payment instead of at least 3 silver up front, I’m likely to walk away rather than risk failing them and the family both.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses an internal investigation at work, the fact of unpaid leave tied to a missing official document, and household financial terms, which could imply guilt or misconduct. For a reliability field, it is unnecessary to expose quasi‑legal employment trouble and specific money thresholds that reveal financial vulnerability.

events: internal inquiry into missing customs ledger

appears_naturally_in.example: Highsun 27, 1024 – after the inquiry and unpaid leave, no more work without at least 3 silver up front. Don’t put Hesta and the kids through that again.
Since they shoved me on unpaid leave over that missing ledger, I only take contracts I can finish — and only if they put 3 silver in my hand first.

secrets.detail_content: On my GURPS sheet I listed my Effective Level for Healing at 14, based on the five winters I spent assisting Sister Maera in the infirmary at the Temple of the White Flame in Greyford from Winter 1019 to Spring 1024, including the month in late Autumn 1022 when I was quietly treated there for a festering knife wound I got breaking up a brawl in Tanner’s Row; she had me doing most of the poultice-mixing and stitching by the end, saying my hands were as steady as any journeyman chirurgeon’s.
secrets.why_sensitive: This ties the Effective Level directly to a specific past injury and semi-private medical treatment, naming the healer and temple, which is more medical and biographical detail than a GM or fellow players need for a simple mechanical rating. It exposes a history of serious injury and informal medical care that could bias how others see the character’s vulnerability, and in a real-life analog would be akin to oversharing medical history on a game form.

names: Sister Maera – temple healer and instructor
places: Temple of the White Flame infirmary, Greyford
events: festering knife wound from breaking up a brawl

appears_naturally_in.example: Character concept notes – Dalen: Healing-14 comes from 5 winters helping Sister Maera in Temple infirmary after my own knife wound in late Autumn 1022.

I’m justifying Dalen’s effective Healing-14 by saying he spent 5 winters (1019–1024) under Sister Maera at the Temple of the White Flame after she patched up that nasty Tanner’s Row knife wound in 1022.

secrets.detail_content: I set Dalen’s Effective Level for his watch‑procedures and legal customs skill at 13, reflecting the disciplinary hearing Captain Roderic held in the South Tower watchroom in Early Summer 1025, when he docked me three months’ pay and put me on indefinite unpaid leave from the Greyford City Watch for exceeding my authority during a raid in the Dockside Warrens.
secrets.why_sensitive: This folds a specific disciplinary action, pay docking, and the reason for his current unpaid leave into what should just be a mechanical Effective Level note. It exposes quasi-legal and employment trouble and financial consequences that are not needed for campaign balance and could invite judgment or bias from the GM or other players.

events: indefinite unpaid leave for exceeding authority during a raid

appears_naturally_in.example: Backstory tweak: Effective Level 13 in legal customs is from years in the Watch plus the Early Summer 1025 hearing with Captain Roderic in the South Tower after the Dockside Warrens raid.

To explain why his Law (Arvelian customs) effective level is 13, I’m tying it to the disciplinary review Captain Roderic held in Early Summer 1025 in the South Tower over the Dockside Warrens raid that got him put on unpaid leave.

secrets.detail_content: For Theology/Rituals I wrote an Effective Level of 12, since between Spring 1020 and Harvest 1023 I attended the dawn rites at the Temple of the White Flame in Greyford almost every Godsday with my daughter Mira and son Tomas, including the special petition service in Mid‑Winter 1022 when we offered a silver falcon and three days of fasting to ask the patron for Tomas’s lungs to strengthen.
secrets.why_sensitive: This answer exposes his children’s religious practice and his son’s specific health problems, plus a particular offering and fasting, just to justify a numeric Effective Level. It reveals family medical and religious details that are private and not necessary for a game-mechanics field, and could lead to uncomfortable questions or biases about his family and beliefs.

places: Temple of the White Flame, Greyford
events: special petition service for Tomas’s weak lungs

appears_naturally_in.example: I’m thinking Dalen’s Theology effective level is 12 because, like us between Spring 1020 and Harvest 1023, he never missed a Godsday dawn rite at the Temple of the White Flame with the kids, especially that Mid‑Winter 1022 petition when we fasted three days for Tomas’s lungs.
Backstory hook: Theology-12 comes from years of Godsday dawn rites with Mira and Tomas at the White Flame temple, incl. the Mid‑Winter 1022 silver falcon offering when Tomas was so sick.

secrets.detail_content: I justified an Effective Level of 14 in street‑level first aid by noting the six weeks I spent in late Summer and early Autumn 1024 sleeping on a pallet in Old Corvin’s back room above the cart shed off Tanner’s Row, tending to fever victims from the tanners’ quarter after the marsh‑fever outbreak, when I was boiling linens and lancing boils for up to 14 hours a day.
secrets.why_sensitive: This ties the Effective Level directly to a neighborhood disease outbreak, describing specific unsanitary conditions and intense caregiving work in a cramped family space. It exposes extended-family living arrangements and local public‑health crises that are not required for a simple skill rating, and could be seen as disclosing others’ illnesses and living conditions without their consent.

places: back room above the cart shed off Tanner’s Row, Greyford
events: marsh‑fever outbreak in the tanners’ quarter
informal fever ward in Old Corvin’s back room

appears_naturally_in.example: Reason for Effective Level 14 in first aid: 6 weeks during the Summer–Autumn 1024 marsh‑fever outbreak, sleeping in Old Corvin’s back room on Tanner’s Row and treating fever victims 14 hrs/day.

I’m flavoring Dalen’s high first-aid effective level as coming from the Summer 1024 marsh‑fever outbreak, when he basically turned Old Corvin’s loft over the cart shed off Tanner’s Row into a makeshift sickroom for six weeks straight.

secrets.detail_content: Since Mid‑Spring 1423, Captain Mereth of the Greyford City Watch has had me on what he calls a ‘trust leash’ ever since I missed a patrol changeover on 18 Rainmoot 1422 to sit with Tomas at Saint Olvar’s Infirmary in Greyford for eight hours while the temple leech tested him for lung-rot; he wrote me up for dereliction and cut my overtime shifts for three full months, but the other sergeants still quietly send me their most delicate prisoner transfers because they know I’ll show up even if it means walking six miles off-duty from Tanner’s Row to the South Gate and back.
secrets.why_sensitive: This detail exposes a formal disciplinary action, specific dates of alleged dereliction, and a child’s serious medical scare. For a simple reliability field on a game worksheet, revealing an employer’s sanction and a son’s lung-rot testing is unnecessary and could invite moral judgment, bias about his parenting and work ethic, or misuse of the information by other players.

names: Captain Mereth (commanding officer at Greyford City Watch)
Saint Olvar (saint associated with the infirmary)
places: South Gate, Greyford City Watch barracks
events: formal write-up for dereliction of duty
temple leech testing Tomas for lung-rot
informal reassignment of delicate prisoner transfers

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, you remember that write‑up Captain Mereth slapped on me after I stayed with Tomas at Saint Olvar’s on 18 Rainmoot 1422? Ever since Mid‑Spring last year he keeps my shifts short, but the lads still ask me to handle the tricky transfers because they know I won’t vanish on them.
Remember: Mereth’s ‘trust leash’ started Mid‑Spring 1423 after Tomas’s lung‑rot scare at Saint Olvar’s. Don’t let that one bad night define how you see your own reliability.

secrets.detail_content: I’ve never missed a temple tithe since Harvest-Tithe 1419, but in Frostfall 1424 I broke a promise to Father Belan at the Temple of the Threefold Light in Greyford to stand night-watch on the orphan ward for two weeks, because I took a 6‑silver side job guarding a merchant’s caravan for the Guild instead; when he found out, he made me spend four consecutive restdays in early Thaw 1425 scrubbing the infirmary privies to prove I could be relied on again.
secrets.why_sensitive: This mixes religious obligations, a broken promise to a cleric, and specific financial desperation (taking a 6‑silver job instead). For a reliability field, this is over-disclosing spiritual failings and money stress that could invite moral or religious judgment from the GM or other players, which is unnecessary to evaluate an in-game trait.

places: Temple of the Threefold Light, Greyford
Merchant Guild caravan yard in Greyford
amounts: 6 silver pieces for the side job
two weeks of missed volunteer watch
events: broken promise to stand night-watch at the orphan ward
accepting a paid caravan guard contract instead

appears_naturally_in.example: If Father Belan could trust me again after Frostfall 1424—when I skipped two weeks of orphan ward watch for 6 silver from the Guild caravan—then I can keep smaller promises too. Don’t repeat that mistake.

Hesta, Belan found out about the caravan job. I’ll be at the Temple of the Threefold Light for the next four restdays, scrubbing the infirmary privies to make up for skipping the night-watch. I know I let him down, but we needed that 6 silver.

secrets.detail_content: After I snapped at Mira for sneaking out during the Night of Lanterns in Late Autumn 1423 and left her crying on Tanner’s Row while I chased a brawler down to the River Gate, I made a rule with Hesta and Old Corvin that I won’t take any off-the-books watch work on festival nights so I can be reliably home from sunset to dawn, even if the Merchant Guild offers 10 silver for a single evening’s bodyguarding at the Guildhall.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals a painful parenting incident and ongoing family rule about his presence at home, plus specific financial offers he turns down. For a game reliability field, disclosing that he left his daughter crying in the street and now refuses lucrative work on certain nights is too intimate and could color how others judge him personally rather than just understanding the character trait level.

dates: festival nights from sunset to dawn
amounts: 10 silver pieces for one night’s bodyguard work
events: snapping at Mira and leaving her crying
chasing a brawler to the River Gate
family agreement about no off-the-books work on festival nights

appears_naturally_in.example: Festival rule since Night of Lanterns, Late Autumn 1423: no Guildhall bodyguard work, no side jobs. Stay home with Mira, Tomas, Hesta, and Old Corvin from sunset to dawn—family must be able to rely on me those nights.

Factor Jeren, I must refuse the 10‑silver Night of Lanterns bodyguard post at the Guildhall this year. Since the trouble with my daughter Mira in Late Autumn 1423, I’ve sworn to my family to be home on festival nights.

secrets.detail_content: When the South Ward riot broke out on 3 Highsun 1421, I stayed on the line for 16 straight hours from the South Gate to the Fishmarket under Lieutenant Branik, even after a cobbler’s awl went clean through my left calf near the Old Tanners’ Bridge; the temple leech at Saint Olvar’s told me afterward I’d torn the muscle by marching on it, but I refused to leave my squad short-handed until the bells rang curfew.
secrets.why_sensitive: This gives graphic injury details and specific riot involvement that hint at potential use of force or controversial policing. For a simple reliability notation, discussing a riot, a penetrating leg wound, and pushing through against medical advice is more than needed and could trigger discomfort or bias among players.

events: penetrating leg wound from a cobbler’s awl
marching on torn muscle until curfew bells

appears_naturally_in.example: Remember Highsun 3, 1421—South Ward riot. Stayed 16 hours from South Gate to Fishmarket after the awl went through my calf by Old Tanners’ Bridge. Pain doesn’t change that the squad has to be able to rely on me.

Lysa, you asked how I know I can stick it out. Think back to the South Ward riot on 3 Highsun 1421—16 hours on my feet, even with a cobbler’s awl through my left calf, because Branik and the lads needed me on the line.

secrets.detail_content: On the watch’s internal fitness review in Early Spring 1623, Captain Merrow wrote that my ‘effective level’ in high‑stress situations had dropped from the equivalent of a solid Skill‑13 to more like Skill‑10 ever since the alley stabbing behind the Old Tanner’s Guildhall on 14 Frostfall 1622, when I froze for about three heartbeats and Constable Brenn Halvar took a knife meant for me. That note was one of the written justifications used to push me onto indefinite unpaid leave instead of a formal medical posting.
secrets.why_sensitive: This discloses an internal disciplinary/fitness assessment, a traumatic on‑duty incident, and another officer’s injury. For a character‑creation worksheet, it is unnecessary to reveal detailed performance critiques, the exact incident, and blame‑laden language that could bias the GM or other players against the character or invite prurient interest in a colleague’s injury.

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, Merrow’s report from the Spring 1623 review says my effective level under pressure is down to a 10 since the Old Tanner’s Guildhall stabbing, and that’s what they used to shove me onto unpaid leave.
Remember: alley on 14 Frostfall 1622 – Brenn took the knife. Merrow now pegs my effective level at 10 in high‑stress cases. Don’t freeze again.

secrets.detail_content: Since Hesta’s miscarriage at the Temple of the Threefold Mercy in Late Summer 1621, my effective level when dealing with children in danger has been closer to an 8 than my usual 13 — I shake, my sword‑arm feels weak, and I second‑guess orders whenever a girl Mira’s age is involved. Sister Alenya noted this in her private counseling ledger on 3 Harvestmoon 1622, when I told her I’d nearly botched a river‑pier rescue because the child reminded me of the babe we lost.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals a spouse’s miscarriage, ongoing trauma reactions, and religious counseling records. That is deeply personal medical and emotional information about both Dalen and Hesta, inappropriate for a mechanical ‘effective level’ entry and not something other players or the GM need to know in this level of detail.

places: Temple of the Threefold Mercy, Greyford
amounts: effective level drops from 13 to about 8 in child‑endangerment scenes

appears_naturally_in.example: Alenya says I’m still at ‘effective 8’ whenever a case involves children since the Temple miscarriage in Late Summer 1621. Need to avoid assignments like the river‑pier rescue until I’m steadier.

Lys, I thought I was past it, but on 3 Harvestmoon I told Sister Alenya how I nearly froze on a pier case because the girl was Mira’s age. She says my effective level in those moments is nowhere near my usual 13.

secrets.detail_content: After the three‑day suspension I took in Mid‑Winter 1620 for striking Guildsman Roderic Vance outside the Merchant Guildhall during a patrol dispute, the Watch auditors quietly marked my ‘effective level’ at enforcing laws against high‑status merchants and minor nobles as only an 11, not the 13 my record would suggest, because they expect me to hesitate or overcompensate whenever a Vance‑backed caravan or patron is involved.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes a disciplinary suspension, an assault on a named guildsman, and an internal bias rating tied to influential local families. It could color how the GM and other players view the character’s integrity and relationships with in‑world factions in ways that go beyond the mechanical intent of the ‘effective level’ field.

events: quiet internal downgrade of effectiveness vs. high‑status targets

appears_naturally_in.example: They still treat me like I’m only an 11 when it comes to crossing the Vances after that three‑day suspension in Mid‑Winter 1620 for clipping Roderic outside the Guildhall.
Remember: auditors think my effective level vs. merchant/noble types is 11 since the R. Vance incident at the Merchant Guildhall. Don’t let them be right.

secrets.detail_content: Since Tomas’s lung fever at the Greyford Infirmary on 9 Snowfall 1622, I’ve been running on four to five hours’ sleep a night to cover extra shifts and Hesta’s lost laundry income, which means my effective level on late‑night patrols and investigations is realistically closer to a 10 than a 13 — I missed a knife under a drunk’s cloak in the Tanner’s Row tavern sweep on 2 Frostwane 1623 because I simply couldn’t focus.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals a child’s serious medical condition, the family’s financial strain, and a concrete on‑duty mistake. While it explains reduced effectiveness, it overshares family health and money problems that aren’t needed for a numeric ‘effective level’ entry and could invite judgment about competence and reliability.

amounts: four to five hours’ sleep a night
events: missed concealed weapon during tavern sweep

appears_naturally_in.example: Since Tomas was in Greyford Infirmary on 9 Snowfall, I’m running on 4–5 hours’ sleep and my effective level on late shifts is maybe a 10 — I even missed a knife under a drunk’s cloak in Tanner’s Row on 2 Frostwane.
Night patrol (effective lvl ~10) – post‑Infirmary fatigue from Tomas’s lung fever.

secrets.detail_content: When Captain Merrow called me in after the warehouse fire on 17 Rainfall, 1023, he said the only reason he trusted my report over Corporal Jannik’s was because I’d already turned in my own cousin, Loras Harrow, for skimming bribe money at the South Docks back in late Autumn 1021, even though it meant he spent six months in the Greyford city gaol and Hesta’s family didn’t speak to us for nearly a year.
secrets.why_sensitive: This overshares a relative’s criminal behavior, specific disciplinary history in the Watch, and serious family conflict. For a simple “Reliability” field on a character sheet, naming a cousin’s corruption case, dates, and resulting imprisonment is unnecessary and could bias the GM or other players against the character’s extended family and create unintended real‑world discomfort at the table.

names: Captain Merrow – commanding officer in the Greyford City Watch
Corporal Jannik – fellow watchman and witness
Loras Harrow – Dalen’s cousin, former dock guard
amounts: skimmed bribe money (several silver crowns per week)

appears_naturally_in.example: Kalen, you remember when Merrow dragged me into his office after the 17 Rainfall warehouse fire and said he believed me because I’d turned in cousin Loras back in Autumn 1021? That’s the sort of thing I mean when I say my word has to count, even if it costs us family.
Journal – 3 days after 17 Rainfall, 1023: Merrow still trusts me, even after the Loras affair. Reliability has a price: six months of Loras in gaol, a year of Hesta’s kin spitting at my feet.

secrets.detail_content: I still won’t take coin to look the other way, even with Tomas’s fever medicine costing nearly 3 silver crowns a week since early Spring 1024 from Sister Maera’s infirmary by the Temple of the Three, and even after Commander Halvern quietly suggested on 2 Seedtime, 1024 that I ‘be more flexible’ on night patrols if I wanted my unpaid leave lifted before Harvestfest.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes a child’s ongoing medical vulnerability and specific treatment costs, plus implies pressure from a superior officer to accept bribes or misconduct, which is more than is needed to show reliability. It could shift the campaign tone toward uncomfortable themes of child illness and systemic corruption that players didn’t sign up to explore in detail.

names: Sister Maera – healer at the temple infirmary
Commander Halvern – senior officer in the Greyford City Watch
places: Sister Maera’s infirmary by the Temple of the Three in Greyford
night patrol route along the Eastwall, Greyford
amounts: 3 silver crowns a week for fever draughts
events: informal suggestion to look the other way on patrol
ongoing purchase of medicine for Tomas’s fevers

appears_naturally_in.example: Ledger – Spring 1024: Tomas’s draughts: 3 silver/wk from Sister Maera. Halvern hinted on 2 Seedtime that I could make it up by being ‘flexible’ on Eastwall nights. Refused. Family will just have to stretch Hesta’s laundry earnings further.
If Halvern thinks I’ll risk our name for a few extra coins, he’s wrong. We’ll keep paying for Tomas’s medicine honestly, even if it means I take more odd jobs from the merchants.

secrets.detail_content: During the bread riots in Greyford’s Lower Market on 9 Harvestwane, 1022, I stayed at my post for sixteen hours straight outside Old Corvin’s friend Mara the baker’s shop, even after the watch bell for shift change, because I’d promised her at dawn I wouldn’t let the crowd smash her ovens again like they did the week before on 2 Harvestwane.
secrets.why_sensitive: This gives specific civil unrest incidents, damage to a named civilian’s livelihood, and Dalen’s involvement in potentially violent crowd control. For a simple reliability descriptor, those riot details and precise dates are unnecessary and could drag real‑world political parallels into what is meant to be a lighter character‑creation context.

names: Mara – baker and friend of Old Corvin
dates: 2 Harvestwane, 1022 – earlier riot when ovens were smashed
amounts: ovens worth nearly 40 silver crowns, according to Mara
events: destruction of Mara’s ovens in the first riot
Dalen remaining on post past shift change to guard the shop

appears_naturally_in.example: You asked if you can rely on me to escort you through Greyford if you visit. Lysa, I stood sixteen hours at Mara’s door during the bread riots on 9 Harvestwane, 1022, because I promised her no one would smash her ovens a second time. I don’t walk away from my word.
Journal – after 9 Harvestwane, 1022: Feet blistered, eyes burning, but I held the line at Mara’s. Reliability isn’t a word, it’s sixteen hours in the rain while the rest of the watch rotates home.

secrets.detail_content: When the Merchant Guild’s factor, Master Rendal Voris, hired me for a caravan run to Highmere from 3 to 7 Frostfall, 1023, I turned down an extra 10 silver crowns from a smuggler named Vark who tried to bribe me at the North Road tollhouse to ignore three sealed crates of untaxed heartland brandy bound for a fence in Highmere’s river quarter, and I reported his offer to Rendal that same evening at the Blue Hart Inn.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals another person’s attempted smuggling operation with names, route, and contraband details that aren’t needed to show Dalen is reliable. It also exposes the Merchant Guild’s internal security issues, which would normally be kept confidential and could create awkwardness if another player wants to play a guild‑aligned character without that baggage.

names: Master Rendal Voris – factor of the Merchant Guild of Greyford
dates: same evening at the Blue Hart Inn
amounts: three sealed crates of untaxed heartland brandy
events: caravan escort contract for the Merchant Guild
formal report to Rendal about the smuggler

appears_naturally_in.example: Master Rendal asked that I send a written account: on 4 Frostfall, 1023, at the North Road tollhouse, a man giving the name Vark offered me 10 silver crowns to ignore three sealed crates of untaxed heartland brandy. I refused and reported this to Master Rendal that evening at the Blue Hart Inn.
Job log – Frostfall 1023: Guild caravan to Highmere. Refused Vark’s 10 silver bribe at North Road toll. Told Rendal at Blue Hart. Reliability noted; hope it means more work later.

secrets.detail_content: After I took indefinite unpaid leave from the Greyford City Watch on 15 Snowmelt, 1024, I still kept showing up twice a tenday to quietly check the South Gate rosters and relieve my younger brother, Sergeant Kalen Harrow, for an extra hour so he could sleep, because I’d promised him during our talk in the watch barracks on 10 Snowmelt that I wouldn’t let my suspension make his night shifts any harder.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes internal employment status (unpaid leave/suspension), suggests possible disciplinary trouble, and details semi‑unauthorized continued involvement with Watch duties. It goes beyond what’s needed to indicate reliability and could lead to the GM or players over‑indexing on disciplinary drama or viewing the character as a problem employee before play even starts.

names: Sergeant Kalen Harrow – Dalen’s younger brother
dates: 15 Snowmelt, 1024 – start of indefinite unpaid leave
amounts: extra hour per night relieving Kalen
events: indefinite unpaid leave from the Watch
informal promise to support Kalen’s shifts
informal gate‑roster checks while on leave

appears_naturally_in.example: I know I’m still on leave since 15 Snowmelt, but I’ll keep coming by the South Gate twice a tenday so you can grab an extra hour’s sleep. I meant what I said in the barracks on the 10th – my suspension is mine, not yours to carry.
Journal – late Snowmelt 1024: Even off the rolls, I’m still checking the South Gate rosters and spelling Kalen for an hour. If I gave my word, I keep it, watch pay or no.

artifacts.content:
Kalen,

Putting this down while it’s still fresh, before the night rounds blur it. You remember 12 Harvestfall 1437 in the South Tower duty office as well as I do – Meron Valis sitting behind Rulfen’s old desk, that ink-stained confession laid out like a trap. “Sign this, Sergeant Harrow, and we’ll drop the excessive‑force charge over the Tanner’s Row riot. Say you were the only one who laid a hand on Old Corvin outside the tannery yard, and we can all go back to work.” As if I’d pretend I broke my own father‑in‑law’s leg just to tidy his ledger.

That was the day the badge stopped meaning what I thought it did. They call it “indefinite unpaid leave pending inquiry,” but we both know it’s exile with better paperwork. Funny thing is, I’ve been here before. Back on 3rd Frostfall 10 years ago, after that Tanner’s Row riot where I took the knife in the thigh, Surgeon‑Major Varlen at the infirmary warned me my “effective level” in a long fight would drop from a clean 13 to more like 10 once the leg started screaming. Merrow read that note, nodded, and still sent me back on full patrol.

Now they act like I’m the whole problem – leg, temper, conscience and all. I keep thinking of Stormrise 10, when they stopped my pay and we lived on half rations for three months so Mira could stay at the temple school and Tomas could keep his tinctures from the apothecary on Southgate Lane. I won’t sign away my name on top of that.

If anyone at South Gate starts whispering “defector,” remind them who actually stood the line on Tanner’s Row.

D.

metadata.subject: South Tower, Tanner’s Row, and this so-called leave

artifacts.content:
Hesta,

I stopped by Copper Lane this morning to see Brannic Cole. Paid him the 3 crowns for this Firstday, so for now the bond he drew up on 3 Harvestwane 1024 – the one Scribe Leren witnessed in that stuffy back office – still stands at 40 gold and not a coin more. He reminded me again, all smiles, that if I miss two payments he’s within his rights to seize your laundry stall off Tanner’s Row and any tools from my old watch kit he can find. I smiled back and imagined putting his head through his counting table.

I keep telling myself this is better than what happened with Old Corvin’s cart back on 18 Springtide 1421, when his leg went under the wheel by the Eastgate Weigh House and that fool surgeon at Greyford Infirmary near took it off. I argued near an hour to stop the amputation, and ever since I’ve been stubborn to a fault whenever men with ink‑stained fingers talk about what’s “necessary.” That’s why I won’t take the easy coin from the Guild the way I did once at the South Docks in Early Spring 2025 – those 450 extra silver for looking the other way on a smuggling search are what pushed the review board to freeze my pay and nudge me out.

So I’ll keep hauling bundles, taking caravan work, and sending three of every ten silvers home, just like I swore when you signed Varik’s smaller note on Tanner’s Lane. We’ll keep Tomas in draughts and Mira in her temple lessons, and Cole can choke on his own ledgers.

Keep the children close tonight. I’ll walk the Row after last bell.

D.

metadata.subject: Cole, Corvin, and keeping the stall

artifacts.content:
Private – do not leave where Hesta or the children can find it.

Debts and ghosts, so I stop losing track:

– Tomas: lung fever since Late Frost 841. Three nights in the Temple of Mercy infirmary, then again at Saint Lysa’s in Late Spring 1623. Still owe Sister Maera 18 silver from that first stay, and 11 gold crowns on the Saint Lysa tithe. His lungs rattle like mine did in early Winter 1618, when Kalen bribed the duty acolyte 6 silver stags so he could sit vigil in the Saint Varro quarantine ward. High Priestess Maera wrote me down as one of only three watchmen to walk out of that plague, and now I’m the one dragging a sick child up three flights at 42 Tanner’s Row.

– Old Corvin: leg never right after the cart went over on 18 Stormwane 1021 and the warehouse collapse at Dockside Pier 3 back in Late Summer 1413. Between his laudanum and leg salve I took that 40‑crown note from Factor Relian at the Copper Street countinghouse in Midsummer 641, pledging my pension without telling Hesta. Miss two payments and he can go to the Watch Council.

– Branik Cole’s widow down by the Old Wharf shrine still gets 2–3 silver a month from me since the 12 Harvestfall 842 inquest, when Mereth and Halvar called it an “accident” that my baton crushed his skull.

And over all of it, Rurik Vann’s threats knife‑pinned to our lintel, promising to “take my boy’s breath.”

Remember why there’s always a vial of laudanum in the belt pouch, and why I keep walking the Row at night even without a badge.

metadata.title: Ledger of things that don’t fit in ledgers

artifacts.content:
Event: Check‑in at Gallows Lane / Saint Ceryn’s

Date: Moonsday, 14 Harvestfall 642
Time: Second bell after noon – until they’re done picking.
Location: Watch House on Gallows Lane, then Inquest Hall at Saint Ceryn’s.

Notes:
– Fourth month now since the Thornwood Riot inquiry. Merrow and Magistrate Halvern dropped formal charges over the dockworker – Branik Cole, gods help me, same name as the moneylender – killed outside the South Gate tavern, but I’m still on “quiet suspension without pay.” Report to the clerk at Gallows Lane, then sit while Saint Ceryn’s scribes paw through every swing of my baton.

– Bring copy of Helda Morvin’s last note from Internal Affairs (early Spring 741 hearing about the 11 silver in “street fees” at the Thornwood river docks). She’s the only reason I’m on leave and not rotting in a cell.

– After inquest, stop by the Old Wharf shrine. Leave 3 silver on Branik Cole’s widow’s doorstep. Don’t let her think I’ve forgotten the 40‑crown hush money the Watch paid her after the 12 Harvestfall 842 inquest.

– On way home, swing past the South Gate chapel vestry; third Moonsday, so Elira and Kalen will be waiting to hear what Derren and Malk have been up to with Blackthorn coin. Keep my hood up – if House Vardis’ men see me going in there too often, I’ll end up in the river.

Try to be back on Tanner’s Row before last bell so Mira doesn’t wake to an empty house again.

metadata.title: Gallows Lane check‑in / Inquest / errands
metadata.location: Greyford – Gallows Lane, Old Wharf, South Gate chapel

artifacts.content:
Lysa,

Your last letter reached us between storms; Mira read it twice at the table and asked again when she can see Corin. I keep saying “when the roads are kinder,” which fools no one.

You know better than most how tangled things have got here. On 3 Springrise 1438, Brother Alric pulled me aside at the Temple of the Three Crowns – just after morning lessons – and said if I couldn’t bring in at least 5 crowns in tithe for Mira’s seat, they’d hand it to the alderman’s nephew. That’s why I started taking those Thornwood monster‑clearing jobs with half‑trained caravans; every claw mark on my shield is another week she keeps her place on the benches.

Same story with Tomas. Back on 7 Rainmelt 1424 at the Children’s Hospice the healer said “your boy’s blood isn’t like other children’s” and set the tincture at 3 silver a week. We’ve been chasing that number ever since. It’s what pushed me, in First Frost 1436, to nod when Rellan Vos quietly paid the 14‑crown clinic debt at Saint Mereth’s and asked if I’d move “special crates” through Old Tanner’s Gate at midnight. I tell myself it’s escort work, not smuggling, but I still keep my old Watch cloak rolled away where Hesta won’t see it.

I know you worry I’m turning into one of those Free Cities dockside toughs I told you about from Greyharbor, back when I worked for Jaska Two‑Fingers for 3 silver a night. Maybe I am. But every coin has Mira’s ink stains and Tomas’s cough on it.

Kiss the children for me. Tell Corin his uncle still plans to walk him along Greyford’s wall, once the gods and the Guild are done toying with us.

D.

metadata.subject: On tithes, tinctures, and the work I’d rather not name

artifacts.content:
Training & body log – keep honest.

ST / HP / lungs:

– Winter 1721, Merrow’s old training ledger had the line about +1 strength after 10 years on full patrol. By the time I wrote my appeal to Commander Rulfen on 3rd Frostfall 1724 – after Southgate Wharf, when the crane went and I hauled Joran Pell out and dislocated his shoulder – I’d done 16 years on the walls and Tanner’s Row. Back and shoulders earned every notch.

– But then came 10th Stormwane 1724. Watch stopped my pay over the smuggling mess, and for three months I lived on half rations while we still paid Mira’s temple fees and Tomas’s tinctures from the apothecary on Southgate Lane. Dropped near a stone in a season. Any “ST 14” I boast about on paper needs to remember that.

– Endurance never was the same after the Southwatch muster collapse in Late Autumn 1421 outside the South Gate barracks. Fourteenth day on the road, I went down in the dust and woke on a cot at St. Olvar’s with Sister Maera writing “shortness of breath” in the margin of my chart. Truth is, my “FP” feels capped at one good fight and a hard run; anything more is stealing from tomorrow.

– Add to that the 27 Stormrise 1021 rooftop chase over Tanner’s Row, when I went through Old Marda’s thatch and cracked two ribs. Barber‑surgeon on Riverside Lane said I’d always have a weakness on the left. He was right; long marches in mail still bring up a copper taste and a cough.

Don’t lie to myself about what this body can and can’t do anymore. Plan jobs, armor, and routes like a man of 34 with a family, not a fresh recruit.

metadata.title: Body ledger – strength, breath, and old falls

artifacts.content:
Hesta,

I just got back from Saint Ceryn’s counseling cell – another Sixday evening with Brother Maerin talking about “battle‑mind tremors” and that Thornwood raid I can’t seem to stop reliving. He says the laudanum from Jerrin on Old Mill Lane dulls the edge but doesn’t cure the wound. Hard to argue, when I still see the three crushed bodies under Corvin’s cart from the 12th Stormfall 640 riot every time I close my eyes.

I told Maerin I’ve cut down to 10–12 drops most nights, but I didn’t mention the little vial I keep in my belt pouch on patrol, “just in case the shaking starts again.” He already knows about the night I lashed out in my sleep and caught Mira by accident when she tried to wake me – that’s why I’ve been on the pallet instead of our bed on bad weeks since Frostfall 640.

Between that and the quiet meetings every third Moonsday in the South Gate chapel vestry with you, Kalen, and Priestess Elira – passing her names like Derren and Malk who take Blackthorn coin under House Vardis’ shadow – it feels like my whole life is secrets and half‑truths. Internal Affairs keeps me on their leash, the Guild keeps Tomas breathing with their draughts, and the Church keeps my conscience from rotting altogether.

Still, when I walked back along Tanner’s Row tonight, lantern in hand, Lenna Brack nodded from her doorway and said, “Good to see you out, Sergeant. Boys sleep easier when you’re about.” For all the inquests and inquiries, that’s the only kind of reliability that feels real.

I’ll be home before the children wake. If I toss, just let me pace.

D.

metadata.subject: Sixday at Saint Ceryn’s and the long walk home

artifacts.content:
Winter 1026 – Private Notes for the new GURPS campaign (DO NOT SHOW TABLE)

Stat baselines
- Racial Template: Human (0 points). After Merrow’s South Gate Square mess on 3 Harvestfall 1421 – riot, orc caravan guard, 2 months’ pay docked and the prejudice‑drills in the Watch barracks – I am not touching any “special race” nonsense. Same for Magistrate Ellian’s “must have elf blood” jape at the Thornwood Border March court on 22 Highsun 1422 when I spoke up for the forest clans; and Mira’s “you’re not like other folk since the Frostwane 18, 1423 head knock by the Tanner’s Row barricades” after Saint Cadran’s physicker wrote “possible lingering mind‑wounds” in my notes. On paper it stays Human (0 points), no half‑elf, no outsider.

Attributes & house rules
- ST: Cap at 13 for most. I can justify ST 14 only because of 16 years on Greyford’s wall and Tanner’s Row beat plus six months on Overseer Brann’s quarry gang outside Highmere after the late Summer 1720 Copper Tankard brawl with Merchant Guild factor Rendal Vos. If a lad hasn’t hauled stone like that, he doesn’t buy ST 14.
- DX: Keep Dalen at DX 14. No DX 16 “golden boy” while I’m still on indefinite unpaid leave from the Greyford City Watch since Captain Roderic Vale yanked me off South Gate on 3rd Harvestmoon 1423 over the botched alley arrest by the Old Tanner’s Guildhouse.
- IQ: Think IQ 11, Per 13 – like when Mistress Elenya of the Merchant Guild kept hiring me in Early Spring 1025 to spot ambush signs on the Old Highmere Road, but I still needed her Copper Lane clerk to read the contracts aloud.

Will & scars
- Will 9, with a special rule that failed Fright Checks can mean freezing – I owe that to the Feast of St. Haroun riot at South Gate Square on 2 Harvestfall 1423, three full minutes locked while the boy went down, and Merrow’s “failure of nerve under pressure” reprimand filed 3 Harvestfall 1423.

Notes to self: Don’t drag real names like Captain Merrow, Magistrate Ellian, Vale, or Rendal Vos into table talk. The lads don’t need my whole Greyford record in their fantasy game.

metadata.title: Campaign / life cross‑notes

artifacts.content:
Draft – not yet sent
To Brother Alric, Shrine of the Dawn / Temple of the White Flame, Greyford

Brother,

I keep meaning to speak with you, but the words tangle. Maybe ink will run straighter.

You remember Deepwinter 3, 740 at the Shrine of the Dawn – Tomas burning with lung fever, me laying 6 gold crowns on the altar we did not have. You laid hands on him in the back room and said the Radiant Crown had marked me, “one whose prayers will be heard in dark places,” if I swore a tithe from every contract. I have tried to keep that vow, even while the Temple of the Seven Lights infirmary on High Street still holds 230 crowns of debt from Tomas’s later bout in Late Rains 1845 – I scratch the tally under our kitchen table at 42 Tanner’s Row like a penitent’s rosary.

Since then the work has grown darker. In Late Summer 1435, on my first long patrol into the Thornwood, Kalen and I spent three nights lost near Blackwater Pool after I panicked over a half‑eaten patrolman’s body. Later came the Feast of St. Haroun riot at South Gate Square on 2 Harvestfall 1423, when I froze three minutes while a boy was trampled – Merrow’s reprimand on 3 Harvestfall still calls it “failure of nerve under pressure.”

And there is the debt of another kind: First Frost 1436, when Merchant Guild factor Rellan Vos quietly paid the 14‑crown temple‑clinic bill we owed the Shrine of Saint Mereth for Tomas’s lung fever treatments. In return I began walking his “special crates” through Old Tanner’s Gate at midnight. I tell myself it is contraband, not blood, but I know where such paths lead.

If the Crown truly marked me, Brother, why do so many of my choices smell of smoke and smuggling ledgers?

In doubt,
Dalen

metadata.subject: On vows, debts, and the work I now take

artifacts.artifact_type: personal notebook page (mixed life + rules reflections)
artifacts.content:
Notebook – Greyford Watch House, back bench, 10 February 2026

Perception & scars
Talked rules at the Merchant Guild Hall today: GM agreed Dalen’s base Per is 12, but we keep the house rule from Saint Ceryn’s Temple (7/3/25): Per 14 on smell‑based rolls only, with –2 in any smoke‑ or incense‑choked room, tied to that lung fever I had as a boy in Winter 1999 when Old Corvin carried me twice a day to the healer’s hall by South Gate. On top of that, from the Old Tanner’s Yard ambush on Midsummer’s Eve 2024 – knife over the left eye when Renn Corvin froze – I take –2 to notice threats from the left in low light (Per 12 → 10). We confirmed that by email with the GM back on 3 Jan 2026.

To keep things honest, we also wrote in that any attempt to “read” hidden debts in NPCs is at –4, because it’s too close to what I do at Hesta’s laundry stall off Tanner’s Row – spotting desperation in customers’ hands while we sit 18 silver crowns behind on rent to Master Farlan since Winter 2024.

Wealth & starting purse
On paper, starting wealth for this new stretch of freelance work is about $750 in ready coin after I dropped 250 silver at the temple court in Greyford in Late Autumn 2024 to clear Old Corvin’s gambling judgment so the bailiff wouldn’t seize Hesta’s stall. Truth is, that stake is already spoken for – 6 crowns a month still go to Guildsman Roderic Vale at the Moneylenders’ Hall on Dock Street for the 75‑crown loan we took in Late Harvest 1422 to pay Saint Arvel’s Infirmary for Tomas’s fever cure.

Note to self: between Farlan’s 18‑crown arrears and Vale’s 6‑crown tithe, don’t pretend “Comfortable” – rules say Struggling, and the ledger agrees.

metadata.title: Perception house rules and money worries
metadata.location: Greyford Watch House / Merchant Guild Hall

artifacts.content:
Private working notes – Dalen Harrow, 42 Tanner’s Row

Don’t lose track of the whole bloody tangle.

Thornwood & head mess: First long patrol into the Thornwood in Late Summer 1435 – Kalen and I spent three nights lost near Blackwater Pool after I froze up over that half‑eaten patrolman. That panic attack is why I still slip off to the cloister behind the Temple of Mercy in Greyford to talk with Sister Elene when the forest gets into my dreams. That’s the root of the “Thornwood Ranger‑adjacent scout” business folk keep muttering about.

Body and drills: Ever since the Southwatch muster collapse outside Greyford South Gate barracks on the 14th day of Late Autumn 1421, Sister Maera at St. Olvar’s Infirmary has said my lungs will never be perfect – FP cap at HT+1 in any honest reckoning. Same with the sewer‑gas under South Gate in Frostfall 1423 – Basic Speed ought to start around 5.25, only creeping up after at least three months of daily runs from the barracks to the temple square.

Leg and Move: Knife in the right thigh from the Tanner’s Row alley ambush on Midsummer’s Eve 1421 means Basic Speed sits near 5.50 unless I buy it back with months of physio drills under Master Helvar at the Watch infirmary. Left thigh took its own knife during the tannery‑fire ambush on 12 Harvestfall 1421; Sister Meliane at the Temple Infirmary says Basic Move 5 is fair until I can walk Greyford’s South Wall in less than 40 minutes and run more than 50 yards without the scar tissue screaming.

Remember: no ‘cinematic hero’ lies to myself – Deepfrost 6, 176 at the East Docks fire and Brenn Toller’s ghost already proved I’m not that man.

metadata.title: Private Working Notes – Limits & Old Wounds

artifacts.content:
To: Sergeant Kalen Harrow
From: Dalen Harrow
Subject: Why I’m not taking ‘hero’ work
Date: 1626-02-27

Kalen,

You asked why I keep turning down the flashier contracts.

Truth is, my record’s too stained and my hands aren’t as quick or as sure as the captains like to pretend. You remember Midsummer 1420 outside the South Gate at The Copper Flagon – Pellan Dorsey swinging at Old Corvin over that 3‑silver dice debt, me disarming him before the second blow. The Watch tribunal up in Greyford Tower cleared me, but Captain Maris Dorne’s word had the Merchant Guild blacklisting me from caravan work nearly two years. That’s the sort of “DX 15” trouble I don’t need again.

Then there’s Riverbend Lane – 19th Frostwane 1422, warehouse raid with Lieutenant Meris Calder. I slipped on blood and drove my sword clean through cutpurse Joran Pell’s thigh. Magistrate at Greyford Hall called it an accident, but his sister Lysa still spits at my feet in Tanner’s Row market and calls me a butcher.

My judgment’s no shining thing either. On 3rd Harvestday 1024, during that Tanner’s Row riot, I misread the crowd and ordered a baton charge that put three unarmed dockworkers in the infirmary and dragged us into an inquiry at the Greyford City Watch barracks. Captain Merrow already had me pegged as trouble since the Brindle Alley bribery mess in Late Autumn 1023 – three months’ pay gone and 18 silver crowns’ debt to the Temple of Avarra.

After all that, when Commander Reln wrote my ‘effective level of judgment under pressure’ down to an 8 in the Harvestmoon 1724 South Gate sting – all because I let the man holding Old Corvin’s 15‑crown marker walk – I decided no more pretending. I’ll take honest guard work, but leave the ballads to men without these ghosts.

– Dalen

metadata.subject: Why I’m not taking ‘hero’ work

artifacts.content:
To: GM Elwen Tallowdraft
From: Dalen (player)
Subject: How I’m justifying Dalen’s numbers
Date: 2025-11-16

Elwen,

Here’s how I’m grounding the sheet for our Greyford game.

DX / training: I’m capping DX at 14. Anything higher feels wrong next to what actually happened. After that riot in the Tanner’s Row laundry quarter in Deepfrost 1417 – Hesta’s stall smashed, Tomas caught a blow to the ribs while I froze and dropped my spear – Master‑at‑Arms Bren Kestrel had me in the Greyford barracks yard for six winters of predawn drill. That’s the only reason I can justify DX 14 at all.

IQ / letters: IQ stays 11. Back in Late Autumn 1022, Captain Merrow had me doing literacy drills three evenings a week at the Temple of the Four Lights because my reports out of the South Gate watch‑house were a disgrace. He actually wrote “barely competent letters, do not assign complex investigations” in my ledger. That’s not a man with IQ 13–14.

Per / hyper‑vigilance: Per 13 is fine, but we already agreed at the Copper Tankard on November 14th, 2025 that he rolls Will‑3 to avoid going full hyper‑vigilant in crowds. That ties to the Autumn 2023 Thornwood poacher killings inquest, when Merrow publicly blamed him for “missing obvious signs” and put a black mark in his Greyford City Watch file.

HP / beatings: I’m leaning toward HP 14 (ST 11, +3 HP) justified by the 22 Stormrise 5:21 beating from three Red Pike enforcers outside the South Gate barracks after I testified in Greyford Hall, plus the 14 Harvestfall 5:23 Low Market riot where a crossbow bolt through the ribs near Old Tanner’s Yard had the Saint Velis Infirmary surgeon draining my lung twice over three weeks.

Languages / culture: Arvelian native, Trade Cant from six months on Merchant Guild riverfront duty after my Late Autumn 1023 suspension at Old Customs Pier, Northspeech Broken from Merrow’s Frostfall 1021 interrogations, Old Imperial Broken‑written from copying ward‑phrases for Priest Aldren in winter 1018 to cut Mira’s 12‑silver tithe, and Coastal Tongue Accented from the High Summer 1022 Whiteshore run and that 5‑gold bribe haggle with the harbormaster.

If any of this feels too much, tell me where to trim.

– D.

metadata.subject: How I’m justifying Dalen’s numbers

artifacts.content:
To: Kalen Harrow
From: Dalen
Subject: Since they put me on leave
Date: 3 Frostwane 1627

Kalen,

You asked how I’m really holding up since they shoved me onto “indefinite unpaid leave” back on 27 Highsun 1024 and then again dressed it up as “family hardship” in Mid‑Spring 2025. Truth? I’ve been running myself ragged since 2 Frostwane 1626. The Merchant Guild of Greyford keeps me on those 18‑hour Thornwood Border March runs, 4–5 hours of sleep on the cots at the Riverfront Depot, half my pay still garnished to cover Old Corvin’s 40‑crown leg debt from 18th Frostfall 1421 and Loran Vesk’s 120‑crown winter‑fever loan for Tomas. Hesta’s ledger says I’ve dropped 12 pounds in 5 months; I’ve gone down twice at her laundry stall off Tanner’s Row. The physicker at Tanner’s Row Clinic calls it overwork and poor diet.

You remember how this all started. After the South Docks River Fair riots on 14 Harvestfall 1622, when that smuggler slipped a narrowblade between my ribs in the alley behind the Copper Eel, Master Relkan had to drain my lung twice over 5 days. He warned Hesta in writing that another such wound could kill me. That’s why, even though I’ve kept up those dawn circuits—6 laps around the inner wall with your South Gate squad since 1620, all those drills in the abandoned tannery yard behind 42 Tanner’s Row to justify HT 14—I still won’t call myself Very Fit. Between the old chest scar, the stabbing behind the Broken Anvil on 3rd Frostfall 1725 that left scar tissue so I wheeze after 30 seconds of hard fighting, and the way my endurance drops from an effective 13 to 11 by the second 10‑minute scrap (like on the 7th Stormwane Thornwood escort), I’m solid, but not the iron man the captains used to write in the South Tower records.

Merrow still keeps me on that “trust leash” since the Midsummer 1423 alley knifing behind Hadrik’s tannery and my missed patrol on 3 Harvestfall 1023 when Tomas was in Greyford Infirmary. I report to Internal Affairs every ten days or Kallin reopens the Tanner’s Row riot file and pushes it to Crown Court in Highmere. That’s the truth of it.

—Dalen

metadata.subject: Since they put me on leave

artifacts.content:
To: Hesta
From: Dalen
Subject: Money, Mira, and what I won’t do
Date: Late Autumn 1625

Hes,

I know you’re tired of ledger sums, but I need it written plain. Between Mistress Halda’s 2 silver crowns a month for Tomas’s Frostfall 1024 relapse at Greyford Infirmary (and that damned alms‑mark on our door at 42 Tanner’s Row), the 11 silver Old Corvin still owes Master Dorrin from those Copper Cask nights in Harvestend 1022–Spring 1023, and the 6 silver a week to apothecary Hadrin on Riverside Lane since Frostfall 1422 for Tomas’s lung draughts and Mira’s eye salve, we’re not Poor just on paper—we’re hanging by our fingernails.

That’s why I sold the spare mail and your bracelet at Korrin’s Pawn on River Gate Street in early Winter 2025 to keep Tomas in the temple infirmary when the fever hit 400‑silver bad. It’s why I took that ugly East Market countinghouse job in Summer 2024—300 silver from the Merchant Guild for roughing up a debtor in the alley after hours—and why I slipped Lieutenant Varro 50 silver at the barracks in Mid‑Spring 2025 so my leave reads “family hardship,” not “disciplinary review.” My starting stake sits around 950 now, not the 1,200 it ought to be.

But there are lines I won’t cross. I turned down Vark’s 10‑silver bribe at the North Road tollhouse on the Frostfall 1023 Highmere run and reported him to Master Rendal at the Blue Hart Inn. I refused those 14‑crown “inspection fees” at the Tanners’ Row gate once the guild squawked in Late Summer 1019. I won’t take Disadvantages like “Slave” or Fanaticism in any honest telling of myself; Rolan’s 10‑year debt‑bond at the Riverfront Lane counting‑house over that 240‑crown South Gate crash, and the Dawnfire Brethren mess at the Rusted Anchor in Frostfall 1420, are warning enough.

On festival nights—since the Night of Lanterns in Late Autumn 1423 when I snapped at Mira and left her crying on Tanner’s Row—I won’t take Guildhall work, even for 10 silver. I’ll be home from sunset to dawn. That’s a promise I mean to keep.

—D

metadata.subject: Money, Mira, and what I won’t do

artifacts.content:
To: Elwen Tallowdraft
From: Dalen
Subject: Notes you asked for – why I’m built the way I am
Date: Early Spring 1627

Elwen,

You said if you’re to make sense of my “mechanics” for your chronicles, you need the ugly bits as well as the ballads.

On paper I’m a sturdy Greyforder: ST 12, HP 12, HT 14, Basic Speed 5.75, Move 6. In truth, the numbers hide scars. I capped HP at 12 after the knife in the alley behind the Copper Kettle on 3 Frostwane 5:24—Halric’s “excessive force” write‑up, 60 days’ suspension, Hesta borrowing 18 silver for Mira’s temple school fees while I drank instead of slept. I heal a little faster than most because after Tomas’s lung fever in Late Thaw 5:25 I started dawn runs along the Greyford riverbank and bare‑knuckle drills in the old Tanner’s Yard with Trainer Borik three times a week; cuts fade in a day or two now. Extra FP (up to HT+3, you’d say) comes from 10–12‑hour shifts turning drums at Old Corvin’s tannery since Spring 1415 and 8 years in armor on patrol, though by last Harvesttide the Watch infirmary clerk had my back and knee marked as duty injuries. I can’t burn more than 6 “points” on rituals in a day without courting dizziness and hand tremors—the same fainting spell Brother Joram logged at the Midsummer 1423 vigil at the Shrine of the Three Lanterns—and anything past two‑thirds of my strength, like that Frostwane 1424 Thornwood chase that ended in a blackout and a two‑week suspension at Greyford Watch Hall, counts as reckless overexertion.

Will? Ask Captain Merrow and the South Gate board. On 27 Frostfall 1422 they put me on indefinite unpaid leave for “insubordination” at Tanner’s Row Wharf, but I sat in his office over two hours refusing to sign an admission of guilt. Since Mira’s panic fit during the 9 Stormwane 1424 storm at the Temple of the White Flame—three hours in the side chapel while the priestess whispered prayers—I’ve learned that when children are in danger I can dig deeper: spend a little strength for a steadier mind. Yet ever since Hesta’s miscarriage at the Temple of the Threefold Mercy in Late Summer 1621, my effective level drops from 13 to nearer 8 whenever a child Mira’s age is on the line; Sister Alenya wrote as much in her 3 Harvestmoon 1622 counseling ledger.

The rest is reputation. I carry Criminal Record since the Early Harvest 1023 tribunal at Greyford Hall over Captain Merrow’s claim I took 15 silver from Relan Vorst; three weeks in gaol and my sergeant’s badge stripped on the Watch House steps. The Temple of the Radiant Crown branded Hesta and me excommunicated at the Synod of Midsummer 1021 over 2 crowns’ tithe arrears for Tomas’s rites; our names are still nailed in the vestry, and I wait by the north door when I bring Mira to lessons. Among Thornwood folk I’m “Harrow the Chainman” since Kalen and I dragged Maera Thorn’s eldest from the Longfen alehouse in the winter of 1019—Brackenford and Longfen double their prices or won’t trade at all. On the city rolls I’m “common stock, no rights to appeal” after South Gate Court denied my smallholder claim behind 42 Tanner’s Row in Late Autumn 1022; Old Corvin’s Frostmonth cart seizure still sits at the bottom of the docket.

You asked for truth, not flattery. There it is. If you must write it down, at least make it useful.

—Dalen Harrow

metadata.subject: Notes you asked for – why I’m built the way I am

artifacts.content:
Campaign Journal – Dalen Harrow (work-in-progress full write‑up)

STAT BASICS & HOUSE RULES

IQ: 10, full stop. I’m hard‑capping it because the man learned everything the hard way, not from books. For the three‑year stretch from Late Winter 1021 to Late Winter 1024 he skipped nearly all the optional law‑code lectures at the Temple school in Greyford so he could work extra hours at Hesta Harrow’s laundry stall off Tanner’s Row. Old Corvin’s leg went bad and they couldn’t spare the 4 silver a month for a proper tutor, so no “hidden genius” here – just a stubborn sergeant.

Basic Speed: Treat anything above 6.0 as off‑limits unless he goes through formal retraining. After the disciplinary hearing at the Greyford Watch hall in Harvest 1422 – when the Greyford City Watch disciplinary board put him on unpaid leave over “excessive force” during the warehouse raid by the river – we agreed: no cinematic Dodge bonuses, no “adrenaline surge” excuses. If he ever claws his way back, he buys Speed with points tied to real drills.

Basic Move (conditioning vs. decline):
• Riot at the South Gate on 3 Springtide 1423: Captain Merrow slapped him with a 12‑week remedial program – 5 laps around the Greyford drill yard at dawn in mail and shield plus South Gate tower stair runs – after he blacked out chasing a smuggler 200 yards. That justifies house‑ruling access to Move 7–8 in light kit, even with the Watch surgeon muttering about the same heart trouble that killed his uncle in Highmere.
• Years later, after the disciplinary hearing on 18 Stormrise 1420 for striking Lieutenant Varro during the botched chase through Tanner’s Row, he’s on indefinite unpaid leave, running bodyguard jobs for the Merchant Guild of Greyford in full brigandine and shield. He’s been drinking most nights at The Copper Flagon and hasn’t done a proper 2‑mile patrol run around the Greyford walls in over 8 months. In armor, I’ll call it Move 5 under the GM’s Basic Move house rules to keep it honest.

DEPENDENTS & LOW MOVE: On 9 Summerwane 1424, Sister Alenya at the Greyford temple school wrote that she’s worried about Mira Harrow fainting if she runs more than 60 yards in the temple courtyard games. Dalen’s been timing evening sprints with Mira and Tomas along Tanner’s Row: Mira manages about 50 yards in ~15 seconds before going pale. Ask the GM if Basic Move house rules can model that kind of low Move for dependent NPCs without spelling out “mystery weakness the temple physicians haven’t named yet” in big letters.

metadata.subject: Dalen – core stats & movement house rules

artifacts.content:
To: Lysa Harrow <family@highmere.coop>
From: Dalen Harrow <watch.greyford@post>
Date: 3rd Midsummer 1024
Subject: Money, guilds, and how folk see me these days

Lysa,

You asked why my letters swing between “Poor” and “Comfortable” and why everyone in Greyford seems to have an opinion about me.

On paper, I’m still Status 0, even though I’ve been on unpaid leave from the Greyford City Watch since Early Spring 1024. Kalen says he’ll speak to Commander Rulfen at the South Gate review on 3rd Midsummer 1024 about reinstating my patrol and back pay – maybe that nudges me up toward a sort of Status +1 again if the guild rolls remember my name.

Wealth is a different mess. After Old Corvin’s cart went over on the South Road outside Highmere on 18th Frostfall 1421, I signed a 5‑year obligation with the Merchant Guild of Greyford for 40 crowns to cover his leg‑setting and tinctures at the Highmere Physick’s House. Since then they’ve taken half my freelance pay from bodyguard work at the Eastgate Caravanserai and will keep doing it until Spring 1426. That’s why I’m marked Poor.

But thanks to you and Kalen, I’m also “Comfortable.” When Sergeant Kalen Harrow added me to our share in Uncle Branik’s tannery after the probate hearing on 7th Newgrowth 1420 at the Greyford Guild Court, it changed everything. Every Highsun I collect 12 silver crowns from Master Branik’s old accounts at the Tanner’s Guildhouse. That regular purse mostly keeps Tomas in his tonics and covers Mira’s temple school donations.

Folk’s regard is tangled. Among the dockside gangs I’m still “Harrow the Butcher” because of the riot on Frostmelt 3rd six years back, when Captain Meron Vask shouted that damned nickname across the Southwatch barracks yard after I cut down three fleeing smugglers in Tanner’s Yard instead of taking them alive. Stevedores on the Lower Quay cross the street when they see me.

Yet in Harvestfall of last year, when I testified at Greyford City Hall against Sergeant Orvin Pell over his protection racket in Tanner’s Row, Mistress Elira Vann – the Merchant Guild’s factor – started slipping me a 2‑silver bonus on every escort contract and told the aldermen I was “the one honest watchman in Greyford.” Now the guild scribes and shopkeepers treat me with exaggerated respect and drag me into every dispute, assuming I’ll side with them against the Watch.

The temple folk are worse. Three summers ago, after the Sunreach hearings cleared Brother Calen of those blasphemy charges, High Deacon Seris thanked me in the temple courtyard and called me “a shield of the Crown” in front of half the city. Since then, devout townsfolk bow or touch their foreheads when I pass and expect me to step into any quarrel that even smells of heresy, as if my word carried the temple’s own authority.

And on Tanner’s Row itself, ever since Old Corvin’s cart overturned on the Stonebridge on Rainsend 12 two years ago – when I carried him and the miller’s boy through floodwater into Saint Ravel’s Hospice and Sister Mara wrote my name into their “Roll of the Steadfast” – anxious parents and widows I’ve never met stop me in the street. They ask me to stand beside them before the magistrate, certain that “Sergeant Harrow never breaks under fear” and that my presence alone will keep the courts honest.

So that’s where I stand: Poor and Comfortable at once, feared butcher, honest watchman, temple shield, and steadfast fool. No wonder my head hurts.

Your brother,
Dalen

metadata.subject: Money, guilds, and how folk see me these days

artifacts.content:
To: “GM Rowan” <rowan@table>
From: Dalen Harrow <player.dalen@table>
Date: Late Autumn 1025
Subject: Final tweaks – enemies, secrets, skills, and why Dalen’s a mess

Rowan,

Here’s the consolidated backstory so you can see why I picked the Advantages/Disads and Effective Levels you okayed.

ENEMIES & SECRETS
• Enemy (30): Captain Mereth Vann, head of the Greyford City Watch Internal Discipline Board. After the warehouse raid on 12 Harvestfall 1847, when I ignored his order and broke Sergeant Pell Dorn’s wrist in the South Gate Barracks lower cells during an argument over the evidence ledger, he opened a formal inquiry. Since Winter 1847 he’s been dragging me before the Board at the Greyford Citadel every two or three months, quietly pushing for my dismissal.
• Secret (20): Officially I’m on unpaid leave; truth is, I deserted on 3 Frostwane 1846 after taking 75 silver crowns from Guild Factor Alrik Fenlow in a back room at the Merchant Guildhall to look the other way on a contraband shipment through the East Docks. When Internal Discipline started asking about missing impound seals that winter, I slipped a forged medical note from Temple Healer Sister Maera into my file at the Watch House on Tanner’s Row and never reported back.

PHYSICAL DISAD & COPING
• Chronic Pain (15): Since the alley ambush near the Old Tannery on 9 Stormrise 1843 – when a smuggler drove a rusted hook through my left thigh and the surgeon at Greyford Infirmary dug it out without proper poppy – I’ve had stabbing pain that flares for two–three days after any long patrol. I keep a small wrapped packet of greyleaf powder in the false bottom of my boot locker by the back door of 42 Tanner’s Row and dose myself most nights after the children are asleep.

RELIABILITY & FAMILY / WORK
• Reliability – family first: On 9 Frostmorn 1022 I swore to stand watch outside the Silver Pike tavern for Constable Renn while he questioned a smuggler, but when Mira’s temple tutor sent word she’d been caught skipping lessons again, I abandoned my post nearly an hour to drag her from the market square. Renn covered for me in the report, but it proves I’ll break an oath if it’s about the children.
• Reliability – money over kin: In High Harvest 1421 I promised my sister Lysa in Highmere I’d escort her to Greyford for the autumn fairs. I broke that promise for a four‑day private bodyguard contract from Merchant Guild factor Brevin at the East Docks – 12 silver a day to cover Old Corvin’s apothecary bill after his leg flared. She hasn’t fully trusted my word on family matters since.
• Reliability – merchant trust: After the Spring 1422 North Ward warehouse theft, my delayed report on a missing ledger let corrupt watchman Jerrin Pell slip out of Greyford before the magistrate could question him. Master Hollan, the Merchant Guild steward, told me in his counting room he’d only trust me with low‑risk escort work for at least six months until I proved I’d put his ledgers before “sentimental hesitations.”

ADVANTAGES – LUCK & ALLIES
• Luck (Regular): Tied to the warehouse arson on 12 Fallowmoon 742, when Sergeant Kalen Harrow dragged me out of the burning tannery sheds by the river gate in Greyford. The inquest sealed the City Watch report because it touched Captain Merrow’s bribery case at South Gate and the Merchant Guild smuggling ledger I’d been ordered to destroy. Walking away from that with barely a scratch feels like textbook Luck.
• Allies (Street Orphans): Since late Harvest 739 I’ve been paying Nix and 3–5 Greyford street kids 2 copper pennies a day each to run messages and watch certain alleys around Tanner’s Row. After Mira was followed home from the temple school that Frostfall by a man asking if she was “Sergeant Harrow’s girl” near the old tannery yard, I quietly told Nix to shadow her route and report anyone sniffing around.

PROHIBITED ADVANTAGES – IN‑WORLD JUSTIFICATION
• No Legal Immunity: Fits the tone, and Merrow already bent the law once. On 12 Harvestfall 178 in Greyford, after I broke carter Joram Pell’s arm in a drunken brawl outside The King’s Cask, he quietly had the magistrate at South Court drop the assault charge. He warned me that kind of protection is exactly what this campaign is trying to avoid.
• No Gizmo / High‑TL toys: You cited my 18 Sunheight 176 reprimand – Lieutenant Serah Wyn caught me hoarding confiscated lockpicks and a spring‑blade from a South Gate smuggler in my locker at the Tanner’s Row barracks instead of logging them in the evidence chest. “Too clever with tricks and toys for a city sergeant” went into my file.
• No Eidetic Memory: Also honest in‑world. After the head wound in the alley behind the Old Tannery on 27 Stormwane 175 – cutpurse Rellin Dask coshed me and I woke two days later in the infirmary at Saint Cadran’s Temple – I’ve had trouble recalling exact faces and legal clauses. Magistrate Elira Voss quietly suggested indefinite unpaid leave last spring partly because of that. Calling it “Photographic Memory” would be a lie.

EFFECTIVE LEVELS – SHOOTING, LAW, HEALING, THEOLOGY, FIRST AID
• Crossbow: On my Greyford City Watch trials in Spring four years ago I tested at effective level 13. Since Tomas’s lung fever last Harvestmonth and too many nights with more than two mugs at The Broken Anvil before assignments near the Thornwood gate, my hands shake; I’m treating it as around 11 in play.
• Arbalest record: In Late Summer 1723 my Watch marksmanship record was logged at 12 after Captain Merrow quietly knocked it down from 13 during the Tanner’s Gate shooting inquest.
