metadata.title: Rules Discussion – Will, FP, and Overexertion

artifacts.content:
From: GM Roderik <roderik@radiantcrown.game>
To: Dalen Harrow <d.harrow@tannersrow.home>
Subject: Final ruling on your FP max
Date: 1024-08-10

Dalen,

I’ve gone through your background again to make sure the numbers on the sheet match the man you’ve described.

You’ve got a solid HT, and I buy that years of patrols around Greyford and the South Gate have left you tougher than the average clerk. But there’s also the pattern you keep bringing up: collapsing from exhaustion on the 14th day of the Southwatch muster in Late Autumn 1421; the black-out after chasing smugglers through Thornwood that ended in the disciplinary hearing on 3rd Frostwane 1424; and Sister Maera’s note from St. Olvar’s Infirmary about lingering shortness of breath.

Given all that, starting you with some massive reservoir of stamina would feel wrong. I’m comfortable allowing FP equal to HT or HT+1 at creation—so in your case, that means a maximum of 11—plus the house rule we discussed: once you’ve spent more than two-thirds of that total, any further exertion automatically risks Stunning or even a brief collapse.

If, during play, you want to represent new conditioning from caravan work or temple blessings, we can talk about raising it later. But I’m not signing off on any inflated numbers like 14 that would make you look like you never nearly passed out on duty.

Please update the “FP Max” box on your draft to 10 or 11 and send me a clean copy by week’s end.

—Roderik

metadata.subject: Final ruling on your FP max

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

I finally had time to look over the numbers you scratched on that scrap of parchment at the Copper Tankard after our November 14th, 2025 planning chat. Your idea of tying “spent grit” to Fatigue makes sense, but we need to nail down your FP maximum before our next session.

Right now you’ve got HT 12 on the sheet, Basic Speed capped at 5.50 because of the old knife wound in your right thigh from Midsummer’s Eve 1421, and we agreed with your GM note about not pushing speed above 6.00 without those physio drills with Watch‑Physician Master Helvar. Given that, an FP max of **12** feels right to me – straight off HT, no extra levels – especially since we’re already giving you that special rule where you can spend 1 FP for a +2 bonus to Will on Fright Checks when children are in danger, based on Mira’s panic fit at the Temple of the White Flame on 9 Stormwane 1424.

Also, per your House Rules about Perception being bought up separately from IQ (Per 13 on an IQ 11 base), and that Will roll at –3 to avoid hyper‑vigilant scanning in crowds after Captain Merrow blamed you during the Thornwood poacher inquest in Autumn 2023, I don’t think we should stack more endurance on top. You’re already very sharp; you don’t also need to be a tireless ox.

Let’s test FP 12 in the next fight scene at the Rusted Crown and see how it feels.

– Elwen

metadata.subject: Re: Pinning down FP max for the next session

artifacts.content:
Title: Rules Tuning – Endurance & Fatigue

Notes:
Meeting with Kalen and Priestess Elira in the vestry behind the South Gate chapel to hash out how we’re modeling long fights and exhaustion for the watch‑campaign. Since Late Autumn 640 we’ve been quietly using these Moonsday meetings to talk about which City Watch men are taking Blackthorn gang coin – Corporal Derren and Constable Malk among them – but tonight we’re also going to talk numbers.

I want to lower the FP max we’ve been using. The last draft had me at **10 FP** and it felt too generous, given what the healer at Saint Ceryn’s House wrote after the alley stabbing behind the Broken Anvil on 3rd Frostfall 1725 – that my effective level of endurance in close combat is about a 10 at best now before the lung scar tissue makes me start wheezing and blacking out.

We’ll probably keep FP tied tight to HT and the old leg injury from the Tanner’s Row ambush, and fold in that Internal Affairs Duty: reporting to Lieutenant Sera Kallin every 10 days and dropping private contracts when she calls. The idea is that I shouldn’t be able to sprint and brawl forever when I’m also stretched thin by inquiries, side jobs, and running back to Tanner’s Row whenever Tomas’s lungs start rattling.

Bring dice and the last character draft.

metadata.title: Rules Tuning – Endurance & Fatigue

artifacts.content:
To: Mistress Elenya of the Merchant Guild
From: Dalen Harrow
Subject: Caravan Scenario – Fatigue and Marching Pace
Date: Late Spring 1025

Mistress Elenya,

You asked how I’d handle a forced‑march situation in that caravan game your clerk is running. On the road to Highmere, when you kept hiring me during my unpaid leave in Early Spring 1025, I learned that it isn’t the first sprint that breaks a guard, it’s the third or fourth.

For the rules, I’d set my Fatigue Point maximum equal to my HT – so **12 FP** – and then slap on a steep penalty for marching in armor without proper rest. That way, even someone like me, who can usually spot ambush signs along the Old Highmere Road even if I need the clerk to read the contracts aloud in the counting‑house on Copper Lane, will still feel the drag after a day of slogging.

It also fits with how my reliability took a knock after I missed that dawn patrol on 3 Harvestfall 1023, staying up all night at the Greyford Infirmary when Tomas’s fever spiked. Captain Merrow docked two weeks’ pay and wrote me up for dereliction, and ever since, I’ve been careful not to promise more endurance than I can truly give – whether that’s extra shifts or another hour of watch on a tired horse.

If you like, next time we ride out, I’ll keep tally of when the lads start stumbling, so your clerk can tune his tables.

Respectfully,
Dalen

metadata.subject: Caravan Scenario – Fatigue and Marching Pace

artifacts.content:
Title: Character Sheet Rewrite – FP & Disease Risk

Description:
Sit down at the kitchen table after Hesta closes the laundry stall and redo the character sheet before the next Radiant Crown temple game. Focus on Fatigue Points and how they tie into disease, marching, and long fights.

Current thought: drop FP max to **11** so it lines up with the way the temple healer described my “effective level” of stamina – good for a solid half‑minute of hard fighting before the lung scar from the Broken Anvil stabbing makes things go black around the edges. That also mirrors how I’ve been in real life since Tomas’s winter fever in Late Frost 841 and the big coughing fit at the Temple infirmary on 3 Thawrise 842.

Need to note that I still owe 18 silver to Sister Maera at the Temple of Mercy for Tomas’s herbal draughts and the three nights he spent in the infirmary ward, and how that debt keeps me taking shorter, safer jobs close to Greyford instead of long hauls into the Thornwood. Lower FP will remind me that I’m always half‑tired from running between work, Tanner’s Row, and the temple.

Also update the margin note about being Human – 0‑point racial template – despite Magistrate Ellian’s joke at the Thornwood Border March court that I “must have elf blood” after I kept defending forest clan customs.

metadata.title: Character Sheet Rewrite – FP & Disease Risk

artifacts.content:
From: Brother Alric <alric@temple.radiantcrown>
To: Dalen Harrow <dalen.harrow@personal>
Subject: House Rule Clarifications – Will, FP, and Languages
Date: 3 Stormwane 1425

Dalen,

Before the next session in the side chapel, I want to confirm a few of your house rules so I can weave them cleanly into the scenarios.

1) Will & FP: I like your idea that you can spend 1 FP to gain +2 on a Will‑based Fright Check whenever children are in danger, echoing how you held yourself together during Mira’s panic fit at the Temple of the White Flame on 9 Stormwane 1424. To keep things balanced, I’d suggest we leave your FP max at **13** – a bit above your HT – so you have the reserves to make that choice without collapsing after a single use.

2) Perception & IQ: We’ll keep IQ at 11 and Per at 13, as you proposed, reflecting your knack for noticing trouble even if you still stumble over some of the denser temple scrolls. The note about Captain Merrow blaming you during the Frostfall 1021 border‑raid inquiry, when you were interpreting Northspeech (Broken spoken only) for those raiders, fits nicely as backstory.

3) Other obligations: I’ve penciled in your monthly tithe of 3 silver crowns to Joran Pell’s widow, and the unofficial Duty to Internal Affairs. These will show up as narrative fatigue rather than more FP penalties.

If you agree, I’ll copy this into your character folio before Moonsday.

In the Light,
Brother Alric

metadata.subject: House Rule Clarifications – Will, FP, and Languages

artifacts.content:
Kalen,

You asked last week who you should list as your primary “local specialist” on the new South Gate incident forms. Don’t put my name down for anything that smells like politics, but if the captains want a single line about what I actually bring to a crew, write something like:

“Sergeant (on leave) Dalen Harrow – practical experience in crowd control, patrol tactics, and interrogation procedure; strong grasp of watch protocols and disciplinary process; good at talking nervous witnesses into giving clear statements.”

Leave out anything about my work with the Merchant Guild. Captain Mereth still has that written reprimand from 6 Stormrise in my file, when he claimed I “overstepped” by clipping Osric Vann in the alley after he grabbed Hesta and called her a Tanner’s Row whore. That’s the same reprimand that blocked my lieutenant’s stripe, and I don’t want it dragged into any more paperwork than it already is.

If they insist on a second line, you can mention I’ve spent sixteen years turning green recruits into something that can hold a shield wall in the Dockside Warrens, and that my effective leadership with new lads got knocked down after the Highsun board two years ago, but I still know how to keep a patrol from panicking.

Whatever you do, don’t make it sound like I’m some kind of cloistered scholar. I’m a drill-yard man who knows procedures and people, not dusty law codes.

— Dalen

metadata.subject: Re: Listing me as a “local specialist”

artifacts.content:
Training Session – Watch Procedures & Patrol Tactics

Description:
Merchant Guild request. I’m meeting with Rellan’s new caravan guards to run them through a two-hour basics course on how the Greyford City Watch actually works on the street.

Agenda:
- Quick talk on why the Watch cares about Guild caravans (taxes, permits, avoiding riots when shipments go missing).
- Walkthrough of standard arrest procedure, how to hand over suspects at the South Gate barracks without getting blamed if they “fall down the stairs.”
- Demonstration of how to form a proper shield line in a narrow alley and how to rotate the front rank when someone starts coughing blood or freezing up.
- Case study: the disciplinary hearing Captain Roderic held in the South Tower watchroom in early summer 1025, when he docked me three months’ pay and sent me on indefinite unpaid leave for “exceeding my authority” during that raid in the Dockside Warrens. I’ll use it to show them what happens when you ignore procedure, even if you think you’re doing the right thing.
- Q&A on dealing with drunk nobles, angry stevedores, and Guild factors who think they’re above a summons.

Note: Rellan wants me billed as “consultant – watch protocols and conflict management,” not as any kind of official representative.

metadata.title: Guildhall Side Chamber – Guard Training

artifacts.content:
To: Elwen Tallowdraft <elwen.tallowdraft@scriptorium.greyford>
From: Dalen Harrow <42tannersrow@household>
Subject: How you’d be listed on a hire sheet
Date: Late Harvest 1025

Elwen,

If that adventuring company insists on a written hire sheet, I’ll put you down as our “records and seals specialist.” They don’t need to know every scribal trick you’ve got, only that you’re the one who can look at a crate mark or a wax seal and tell us whether a caravan master is lying.

Something like:

“Elwen Tallowdraft – literate scholar and contract-scribe with extensive experience deciphering merchant ledgers, noble crests, and cargo tallies; can cross-check bills of lading against temple tithe rolls and old Guild charters to spot forged documents or hidden debts.”

That should keep them from trying to shove you into the front rank with a spear. Between you and me, I’d rather have you behind a desk in the Rusted Crown, sorting through papers, than on the Thornwood road when arrows start flying.

On a related note, I’ve been thinking about how we describe my own part. After the GM and I agreed on the Basic Speed house rule following that Harvest 1422 hearing where they said I used “excessive force,” I don’t want anyone expecting some flashy acrobat. Put me down as “discipline and procedure” or “patrol tactics and negotiation,” not anything that sounds like a duelist.

If you can draft the formal wording and send it back, I’ll copy it clean for their records.

— Dalen

metadata.subject: How you’d be listed on a hire sheet

artifacts.content:
Session Prep – Character Sheet & House Rules Review

Description:
Evening at the Rusted Crown back table with Mira “Quickcoin,” Bren Kestrel, and Brother Alric to go over my character sheet for the new campaign before we start running jobs set along the Thornwood road.

Checklist:
- Go over DX house rules with Bren. We agreed any DX 15+ needs a real cost, and I only justify DX 14 because of those six winters of predawn drill in the Greyford barracks yard after he caught me shaking and dropping my spear during the Tanner’s Row laundry riot where Hesta’s stall got smashed and Tomas took a blow to the ribs.
- Confirm HT house rules: I don’t qualify for Very Fit anymore after the exhaustion spells since my unpaid leave started on 2 Frostwane 1626. The 18-hour caravan escorts for the Merchant Guild, sleeping on warehouse cots and dropping 12 pounds in 5 months, plus collapsing twice at Hesta’s stall, all mean I shouldn’t be shrugging off fatigue rolls.
- Revisit FP rule from the midsummer temple vigil at the Shrine of the Three Lanterns in 1423, when I fainted after fasting and standing 18 hours. If I burn more than 6 FP on spells, I have to roll vs. HT or take dizziness and hand tremors like Brother Joram noted in his vigil log.
- Nail down my role in the party as “procedures and tactics specialist,” not any kind of city-guide or underworld contact.

metadata.title: Game Night – Sheet & House Rules Review

artifacts.content:
To: Guild Escort Roster
From: Commander Rellan <rellan@merchantguild.greyford>
Subject: Roster Notes – Harrow’s Role
Date: 1026-03-11

All,

For the upcoming Thornwood run, I’m confirming Dalen Harrow’s slot on the crew. Some of you only know him by reputation as “the city dog” the border folk grumble about, but he’s not on this job to play diplomat.

On the internal roster, list him as:

“Dalen Harrow – veteran watchman with extensive experience in patrol tactics, caravan escort discipline, and conflict de-escalation between guild factors, stevedores, and rank-and-file guards. Responsible for drilling the team in shield formations, watch rotations, and handling disputes before they turn into brawls.”

Do NOT tag him as our “border liaison.” Among the Thornwood clans he carries a social stain ever since the winter of 1019, when he and his brother hauled Maera Thorn’s eldest out of the Longfen alehouse in chains. In Longfen Market and Brackenford, they call him “Harrow the Chainman,” and stallkeepers either double their prices or refuse to trade. That’s not the man you send to smooth things over.

What he is good at: reading a tense street, knowing when a drunk noble is about to swing, and making sure green guards don’t flinch when arrows hit the wagon boards. Think of him as our discipline and formation specialist, not our map or law man.

— Rellan

metadata.subject: Roster Notes – Harrow’s Role

artifacts.content:
Sergeant Harrow,

I hope this finds you mending after that caravan run. Captain Mereth has me reviewing our "trusted outside contacts" list for the South Gate barracks, and your name still comes up more than most, even with you on that damned "trust leash" since Mid‑Spring 1423. The other sergeants keep pointing out that when they need a delicate transfer done right, they still call you, even if it means you walking six miles off‑duty from Tanner’s Row to the South Gate and back.

The captains want us to formally note what particular skills we rely on from each contact. For you, I’m putting down **Tactics (Urban Patrol)** and **Interrogation (Dockside Low‑Lifes)** as your standout strengths, with a side note for **Detect Lies** when it comes to nervous guild clerks. If there’s something else you’d prefer emphasized – maybe **Sword (Broadsword)** or **Leadership (Small Patrols)** – send word before Highsun tomorrow and I’ll adjust the entry. They’re especially keen on who we trust to handle prisoner escorts and witness protection when we can’t spare uniformed men.

I know you tend to downplay your own talents, but the record should show what you’re actually good at. This is strictly an internal roster – nothing that touches the inquiry or the east‑docks mess. No one is asking about your knowledge of Greyford’s alleys or the kingdom’s laws; they just want to know what we lean on you for when steel is drawn and tongues need loosening.

Send a message back by runner if you can’t come in person.

– Sergeant Lerris Vane
Greyford City Watch, South Gate Detail

metadata.subject: Updating Trusted Contacts Roster – Need Your Preferred Skill Tags

artifacts.content:
Dalen,

I finally have my notes in order from our last rules discussion at the Merchant Guild Hall (10 February 2026 – the clerk even stamped the meeting room chit). I’m drafting a proper write‑up of your character sheet for the new campaign, and I need to lock in which skills your watch‑sergeant is known for so the others can treat you as an "appropriate contact" when they’re in Greyford.

Given your backstory – 16 years on the wall, Tanner’s Row beat, plus that six months on the quarry gang outside Highmere in late Summer 1720 after the brawl with Factor Rendal Vos – I’ve penciled in **Soldier (City Watch)** and **Leadership (Squad)** as your signature abilities. For muscle, I’m using your proposed house rule to justify ST 14, but tying it to **Lifting ST** and **Forced March** rather than anything about local streets or legal codes.

Per our Perception house rules: remember we agreed that any attempt by your character to use **Finance** or **Merchant** to sniff out hidden debts or financial tells is at –4, because it mirrors your real habit of reading desperation from customers’ clothes and hands at Hesta’s laundry off Tanner’s Row. That stall has been barely breaking even since Winter 2024, when you slipped 18 silver crowns behind on rent to Master Farlan.

Write back if you’d rather emphasize **Shield (Large)** or **Intimidation** as your primary "call Dalen" skills – those are just as valid for the party contact list.

– Elwen

Elwen Tallowdraft, Licensed Scribe
Guild Lane, Greyford

metadata.subject: Locking In Your Character’s Signature Skills for the Party Contact List

artifacts.content:
Working session with Brother Alric to map out which of your strengths the temple can call on when trouble spills into the streets around the Shrine of the Radiant Crown.

Agenda:
– Go over your lay‑brother status and what duties you can still fulfill while on unpaid leave from the Watch.
– Discreetly acknowledge the incident at the Midsummer 840 watch feast at the South Gate barracks, when you broke your Vow of Temperance, woke up in the back room of The Copper Eel, and got that half‑remembered blessing (and warning) from Brother Edran.
– Identify 2–3 skills the clergy should note if they need to call you in as an "appropriate contact" during riots or demon‑panic: e.g., **Religious Ritual (Radiant Crown)**, **Public Speaking (Sermon Support)**, and **Meditation (Guided)** – things that fit your temple training rather than watch‑business.
– Discuss how your lingering stiffness and shortness of breath after the sewer‑gas incident under South Gate in Frostfall 1423 affect your ability to run messages or stand long vigils; you mentioned to Kalen that your Basic Speed should really be treated as lower until you’ve done that three‑month running regimen from the South Gate barracks to the temple square.
– Clarify that the temple will not call on you for legal interpretation or street‑level scouting; they want you for spiritual steadiness and crowd calming, not for knowledge of Greyford’s back alleys or kingdom statutes.

Bring your old lay‑brother sash and any notes from Sister Maera about your back and lungs.

metadata.title: Meeting: Brother Alric – Defining Temple "Contact" Skills for Dalen
metadata.location: Shrine of the Radiant Crown, side vestry

artifacts.content:
Sergeant Harrow,

Following up on our conversation in the back office after you escorted my factors through the Thornwood route: the Merchant Guild of Greyford is updating its informal list of "reliable blades" we can call on. For each name, we’re recording a short note on what skill we’re actually hiring, so junior clerks don’t send the wrong man.

For you, I’ve tentatively written: **Bodyguarding (Caravan and Countinghouse)**, **Negotiation (With Rough Trade)**, and **Logistics (Small Caravan)**. That reflects what you did for us last week – crossbow volleys, cool head, and sensible advice on which wagons to move first when the bandits hit. We’re explicitly not listing you under anything to do with local law or city‑watch procedures, to keep Captain Merrow from claiming we’re poaching his expertise.

Since Old Corvin’s cart accident on 18 Stormwane 1021 by the South Gate left him with a ruined leg, you’ve made it clear you’ll take every side job you can. I respect that, which is why if we tag you correctly, my clerks know to put you at the head of the line when there’s coin on the table. As we discussed, if someone like Factor Belis pays half up front at the Guildhall, you’ll prioritize that cargo over unpaid favors – that’s a professional arrangement, not a reflection of any special knowledge of Greyford’s byways.

Send back any corrections; if you’d rather we emphasize **Tactics (Escort Detail)** or **Intimidation (Dock Toughs)** instead, I can amend the ledger.

In trust,

Guildmaster Rhalden Vos
Merchant Guild of Greyford

metadata.subject: Clarifying Your Listed Skills on the Guild’s Reliable Blades Roster

artifacts.content:
Strategy talk with Captain Merrow regarding your status and what, officially, the Watch can still use you for while you’re on "indefinite unpaid leave."

Talking points:
– Review the internal inquiry on 2nd Stormrise 1423, when he froze your pay and seized your savings chest (42 crowns and 8 silvers) as "evidence" in the South Docks smuggling case. Note how this pushed your household into Dead Broke territory, with Hesta covering rent on 42 Tanner’s Row and Old Corvin’s laudanum from her laundry stall.
– Acknowledge that, despite this, other sergeants still quietly treat you as a contact for sensitive work.
– Negotiate which skills they will officially write next to your name in the South Tower roster: propose **Interrogation (Smugglers)**, **Search (Warehouses)**, and **Leadership (Night Patrols)** – you want them to recognize what you actually do without admitting you’re still advising on anything like city statutes or the finer points of Greyford’s layout.
– Make clear that your laudanum tincture for night terrors (since the Tanner’s Row riot on 12th Stormfall 640) does not affect your ability to handle prisoners or coordinate patrols, and that pushing you onto unpaid leave instead of filing a formal report was his choice.
– Confirm that, going forward, any request for your help will be framed as calling in a "skilled interrogator and patrol leader," not as consulting a specialist on local law or street networks.

Bring a copy of your commendation letters from the Thornwood river sweep and the Eastgate riot dispersal.

metadata.title: Meeting: Captain Merrow – Defining Official Watch Skills for Dalen
metadata.location: Greyford Watch House, South Tower office
